feed.title: How Investors Are Reacting To Lamb Weston (LW) Losing S&P 500 Status Ahead of Q3 2026 Results
feed.summary: Lamb Weston Holdings is set to report its fiscal 2026 third-quarter results on April 1, 2026, and will be removed from the S&P 500 index before trading on March 23, 2026. While the index removal may cause short-term trading volatility, the core investment narrative remains focused on frozen potato product demand, cost management, and customer traffic trends. Investors should closely watch the upcoming earnings report for details on profitability and leadership's progress on cost savings.

feed.title: Does Clorox’s (CLX) Steady Dividend Mask Deeper ERP And Acquisition Integration Risks?
feed.summary: The Clorox Company recently declared a quarterly dividend of US$1.24 per share, shortly after reporting mixed quarterly results and flagging challenges related to an ERP transition, retailer inventory drawdowns, and acquisition risks. While the dividend remains steady, these operational hurdles, particularly concerning the GOJO acquisition, could significantly impact Clorox's fiscal 2026 performance and its investment outlook. Investors should carefully consider these risks despite the consistent dividend payout.

feed.title: Is Fastenal (FAST) Pricing In Too Much Growth After Its Strong Multi‑Year Run?
feed.summary: Fastenal's stock performance has seen significant gains over the past five years, but current valuation checks suggest it may be overvalued. A Discounted Cash Flow analysis indicates the stock is about 112% above its estimated intrinsic value, and its P/E ratio of 42.40x is well above the industry average and Simply Wall St's Fair Ratio of 32.82x. The article explores bull and bear case scenarios, offering different fair value perspectives based on varying assumptions about revenue growth and operational factors.

feed.title: PriceSmart Expansion To 60 Clubs Tests Rich Valuation And Growth Assumptions
feed.summary: PriceSmart plans to open four new warehouse clubs in 2026, increasing its total footprint to 60 locations and potentially reshaping its growth profile and capital needs. The stock has seen significant returns, up 65.1% over the past year, but Simply Wall St's valuation suggests shares are trading 42.5% above estimated fair value and 1.5% above analyst targets. Investors will be watching new store productivity, member growth, and regional mix, especially given flagged risks like insider selling.

feed.title: Grocery Outlet’s six stores in Contra Costa County not included in list of 36 closing so far
feed.summary: Grocery Outlet announced plans to close 36 underperforming stores nationwide, including nine in California, following a challenging financial year in 2025. However, it was confirmed that none of the six Grocery Outlet stores in Contra Costa County, California, including locations in Antioch, Brentwood, and Oakley, are on the current list of closures. The company is implementing an "Optimization Plan" to improve profitability and operational execution, which involves store closures and reshaping its growth strategy.

feed.title: Five people arrested in connection with Home Depot theft operation in Cedar City
feed.summary: Five individuals were arrested in Cedar City, Utah, for their involvement in a large-scale theft operation targeting Home Depot. Police discovered a significant amount of stolen merchandise at a property after investigating multiple thefts over the past month. The suspects are facing felony theft charges, with one informant revealing that multiple employees of a company were regularly involved in these retail thefts.

feed.title: Kroger (KR) One Off US$2.8b Loss Puts 0.7% Margin Narrative Under Scrutiny
feed.summary: Kroger's FY 2026 results showed a significant one-off loss of US$2.8 billion, leading to a net margin of 0.7% compared to 1.8% in the previous year. This volatility and compressed margin raise concerns about profitability, especially given the consensus among analysts about rising labor and investment costs. While bulls point to forecast earnings growth and a dividend, the high P/E ratio and debt levels mean investors should closely examine the durability of its growth path.

feed.title: Sheinbaum Urges Coca-Cola to Increase Use of Mexican Cane Sugar
feed.summary: President Claudia Sheinbaum has urged The Coca-Cola Company to use more Mexican cane sugar in their soft drinks instead of imported fructose, aiming to support the domestic sugar industry. This proposal aligns with efforts to strengthen national agricultural supply chains and addresses concerns about low sugar prices and rising sweetener imports. Sheinbaum also discussed water management and environmental impact with Coca-Cola executives, emphasizing that new industrial plants must adhere to strict environmental regulations.

feed.title: Chili's Is Winning on Value, Yet Its Parent Company's Stock Still Looks Cheap
feed.summary: Chili's, the main brand of Brinker International, has significantly improved its operational profitability, with restaurant-level profit doubling since three years ago. This success is attributed to its value-driven "3 For Me" menu, which has attracted consumers looking for affordable full-service meals over fast-casual options, leading to strong traffic growth. Despite these improvements and trading at a significant discount compared to its casual-dining peers, the stock's near-term performance faces potential headwinds as it laps high comparable sales growth from the previous year.

feed.title: Final days to visit ‘prime’ McDonald’s location before it closes doors for good after 40 years
feed.summary: A McDonald's restaurant in Red Mill Shops, Derwood, Maryland, described as a "prime" location by customers, is closing its doors permanently on March 15 after 40 years of operation. This closure follows a recent trend, including another McDonald's in Ocean City, New Jersey, that shut down in December. The article notes the broader struggles of the restaurant and retail industry post-pandemic, though the specific reason for the Maryland closure remains unclear.

feed.title: Gap Inc. Ditched Fast Fashion Trends and Hit $15.4 Billion — Posting Its Best Margin in 25 Years, But Still Missed Earnings Forecast
feed.summary: Gap Inc. achieved significant financial success in fiscal year 2025, reaching $15.4 billion in net sales and an operating margin of 7.3%, its best margin in 25 years. This turnaround is attributed to a strategic shift away from chasing fast fashion trends and focusing on operational rigor, brand identity, and inventory management. Despite this strong performance, the article's headline suggests the company still missed its earnings forecast.

feed.title: Will Domino's $9.99 Basketball Promo and Cash Returns Shift Domino's Pizza's (DPZ) Resilience Narrative?
feed.summary: Domino's Pizza recently reported strong Q4 and full-year 2025 results, including increased revenue and earnings, a higher dividend, and a significant share repurchase program. Concurrently, the company is implementing a "Best Deal Ever" US$9.99 pizza promotion during the basketball tournament to drive traffic. This article examines how this promotion, alongside ongoing cash returns to shareholders, could influence Domino's investment narrative and future earnings resilience, especially in a competitive and sometimes sluggish pizza market.

feed.title: Oxford Industries Selects Elm AI to Bring Artificial Intelligence to Responsible Sourcing Across Global Supply Chain
feed.summary: Oxford Industries has partnered with Elm AI to leverage artificial intelligence for responsible sourcing across its global supply chain, including brands like Tommy Bahama and Lilly Pulitzer. The Elm AI platform will automate the analysis of complex supplier data, such as social audit reports and environmental assessments, to enhance due diligence and identify risks more efficiently. This collaboration aims to streamline supplier data management, reduce manual review, and enable Oxford's corporate responsibility team to focus on strategic decision-making and driving improvements.

feed.title: Is Tractor Supply (TSCO) Pricing Look Stretched After Recent Share Price Weakness
feed.summary: This article analyzes Tractor Supply (TSCO) stock valuation using Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) and Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio models. Both methods suggest that TSCO, currently trading at US$50.16, appears overvalued, with the DCF model indicating a 14.5% overvaluation and the P/E ratio exceeding its calculated Fair Ratio. The article encourages investors to develop their own "Narratives" on Simply Wall St's platform to align valuation with personal assumptions.

feed.title: Lululemon Fell 19% in 2026: Do Analysts See Recovery?
feed.summary: Lululemon's stock (LULU) has dropped significantly, trading more than 50% below its 52-week high due to a U.S. sales slump, CEO departure, proxy fight, and tariff headwinds, with Q4 earnings expected to show a 23% EPS collapse. Despite concerns, analysts still see a 22.5% upside, largely driven by strong international growth and anticipated product resets, though a valuation model suggests potential P/E compression. The upcoming March 17 earnings report is crucial for confirming the effectiveness of the turnaround strategy.

feed.title: A2Z Launches Its Smart Carts with NTUC FairPrice, Singapore’s Largest Supermarket Chain
feed.summary: A2Z Smart Technologies Corp. announced a pilot program for its Cust2Mate Smart Carts with NTUC FairPrice, Singapore’s largest supermarket chain. The smart carts will arrive at a new FairPrice luxury store on July 15, 2022, and will be fully available to shoppers by September 1, 2022. These carts integrate with the FairPrice ecosystem to provide a seamless in-store digital shopping experience, featuring in-cart payment and in-store promotion guidance to manage inventory efficiently.

feed.title: Kontoor Brands Posts Record 2026 Revenue: Is a Breakout to $87 on the Horizon?

The success is attributed to the Helly Hansen acquisition and the "Project Jeanius" efficiency program. Analysts see the stock as undervalued, with a target price of $87.89, driven by multi-brand synergies and capital allocation strategies.

feed.title: Krispy Kreme is giving away free doughnuts for Daylight Saving Time
feed.summary: Krispy Kreme is offering a special deal for Daylight Saving Time, providing a free dozen Original Glazed doughnuts with the purchase of any dozen. This offer is available on Monday, March 9th, in-store or through the Krispy Kreme app and website using the code SPRING. The promotion aims to sweeten the transition despite the lost hour of sleep due to the time change.

feed.title: Kroger Is Closing Stores: See the Updated List That Shows Shuttered Locations Across the Country
feed.summary: Kroger has been closing stores as part of a "footprint optimization plan" announced last June, with approximately 33 locations already shuttered and more confirmed for this year. The closures affect various banners owned by Kroger across the country, though the company has not released an official list of impacted stores. This strategy is expected to provide "modest financial benefit" and has implications for employee jobs, as Kroger operated 2,731 supermarkets and employed 409,000 people as of early 2025.

feed.title: Best Buy Pivots to High-Margin Digital Growth: Why $77 Could Be the Next Stop
feed.summary: Best Buy (BBY) is transitioning its business model to focus on high-margin digital growth, leveraging initiatives like its digital Marketplace and Best Buy Ads. Despite a slight miss on Q4 2026 revenue, the company exceeded EPS and EBITDA expectations, demonstrating strong operational profitability. Analysts anticipate a target price of $77.75, an attractive 17.89% potential return, driven by these strategic shifts and the impending consumer upgrade cycle spurred by AI-integrated hardware.

feed.title: Ross Stores Surges on Blowout Earnings: Could This Spark a Run to a $213 Target?
feed.summary: Ross Stores (ROST) experienced a significant surge in stock price following a strong fourth-quarter earnings report, exceeding revenue and EPS estimates. The company demonstrated resilience in its off-price retail model, driven by increased customer traffic and transactions, and announced a new $2.55 billion stock repurchase program and a 10% dividend increase. Analysts project a potential 8.15% return to a street target of $213.75, based on Ross Stores' disciplined inventory management and aggressive expansion plans for 110 new stores in the coming year.

feed.title: AutoZone Powers Through Q4 Headwinds: How Analysts Justify a $4,209 Target
feed.summary: AutoZone (AZO) reported a complex fiscal second-quarter 2026 earnings, with revenue slightly missing estimates but adjusted EPS beating them despite negative impacts from a $59 million LIFO charge and severe winter storms. Analysts project a $4,209.04 target price, representing a 15.7% potential return, fueled by the company's "Mega-Hub" strategy, aggressive expansion, and stock repurchases, with expectations of increased demand after winter. The company is actively transforming its unit economics to dominate the DIY and commercial auto parts markets by leveraging massive supply chain investments.

feed.title: Had You Invested $1,000 in Coca-Cola or PepsiCo 10 Years Ago, Here's What You'd Have Today
feed.summary: Over the last decade, an investment in Coca-Cola would have yielded a better return than PepsiCo, with Coca-Cola returning +140.27% compared to PepsiCo's +120.04%. Coca-Cola's success is attributed to its asset-light franchise model, focus on brand marketing, and the growth of its Zero Sugar product line while PepsiCo faced challenges from a Rockstar write-down and decreased snack volume due to GLP-1 drug adoption. Both companies are Dividend Kings, offering consistent dividend increases, though neither matched the S&P 500's performance over the same period.

feed.title: American Airlines expands digital wallet integration for seamless traveller experience

This initiative is part of American's mobile-first strategy, which also includes enhancements to its mobile app for improved trip management and disruption handling. The airline aims to offer customers more control and convenience by streamlining access to critical travel information.

feed.summary: Kroger's Q4 2025 financial report shows mixed results with revenue of $34.73 billion falling short of market expectations, though earnings per share on a GAAP basis exceeded forecasts. Adjusted EBITDA missed projections, but operating margin remained consistent and free cash flow margin improved. The company's 2026 earnings guidance aligns with analyst estimates, and while same-store sales growth matched the prior year, total sales over the past twelve months are near levels seen three years ago due to limited expansion.

feed.title: J Balvin remade Van Halen's 'Jump' as Coca-Cola's World Cup anthem – with a new Spanish verse
feed.summary: J Balvin has re-recorded Van Halen's iconic song "Jump" to be Coca-Cola's anthem for the FIFA World Cup 2026, featuring artists like Travis Barker, Amber Mark, and Steve Vai. Balvin contributed a new, Spanish-language verse to the track, aiming to infuse it with Latin passion for "fútbol" (soccer). The song and its anime-style video were released by Coca-Cola's new in-house label, Real Thing Records, in partnership with Capitol Records.

feed.title: Lamb Weston (LW) to Join S&P 600 in Upcoming Index Rebalance
feed.summary: Lamb Weston (LW), a leading producer of frozen potato products, is set to join the S&P 600 index before market open on Monday, March 23, 2026, as part of a routine quarterly rebalancing. This inclusion acknowledges the company's strong market performance and potentially influences investor perception. While Lamb Weston demonstrates impressive financial performance with significant revenue growth and profitability, concerns exist regarding its debt levels and asset efficiency.

feed.title: Beyond drops ‘Meat’ from name as it broadens ambitions for plant-based proteins
feed.summary: Beyond Meat is rebranding to Beyond The Plant Protein Co., reflecting its expanded portfolio beyond plant-based meat into other protein categories like beverages. This change comes as the company seeks to meet a wider range of consumer protein needs and diversify its offerings amidst a decline in plant-based meat sales. Despite the name change, Beyond states it remains committed to leadership in plant-based meat.

feed.title: Hooker Furnishings declares $0.115 quarterly dividend By Investing.com
feed.summary: Hooker Furnishings Corporation announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.115 per share, payable on March 31, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 16, 2026. The company, which has maintained dividend payments for 26 consecutive years, offers a dividend yield of 3.37%. Despite revenue challenges and a recent significant earnings miss, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock appears undervalued, and the company is also expanding its board through a cooperation agreement with Global Value Investment Corporation.

feed.title: VF Corporation Shares Signal Potential Market Reversal
feed.summary: VF Corporation is demonstrating renewed strength through operational improvements and a significant technical breakout, even as the broader retail sector faces challenges. The company surpassed analyst estimates for revenue and EPS, providing fundamental support, and its stock price climbed above its 200-day moving average. Upcoming financial reports will be crucial to confirm a sustained recovery and lasting profitability in a volatile retail landscape.

feed.title: Reborn Coffee Adds New Co-CEO
feed.summary: Reborn Coffee Inc. has appointed Jung Jae Lim as Co-Chief Executive, sharing the top role with founder Jay Kim. Lim, who previously joined the board in October, brings over 20 years of experience in international logistics and supply chain management. His expertise is expected to contribute to Reborn Coffee's scaling and long-term value creation.

feed.title: (CNK) Movement Within Algorithmic Entry Frameworks
feed.summary: The article analyzes Cinemark Holdings Inc. (NYSE: CNK) using algorithmic entry frameworks, highlighting a strong near and mid-term sentiment despite a weak long-term outlook. It details specific trading strategies, including position, momentum breakout, and risk hedging, along with multi-timeframe signal analysis and risk management parameters. The analysis identifies a significant 45.5:1 risk-reward short setup.

feed.title: Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) Director Reyne Wisecup Sells 36,920 Shares
feed.summary: Fastenal Director Reyne Wisecup sold 36,920 shares of the company's stock on March 5th for approximately $1.75 million, reducing her position by 48%. The sale leaves her with 40,000 shares. This transaction follows Fastenal's recent quarterly earnings report, where it met consensus EPS estimates and announced an increase in its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: BBWI Deadline: BBWI Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Bath & Body Works, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: Rosen Law Firm reminds investors of Bath & Body Works, Inc. (NYSE: BBWI) who purchased securities between June 4, 2024, and November 19, 2025, about the approaching lead plaintiff deadline of March 16, 2026, in a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Bath & Body Works made materially false and misleading statements regarding its business strategy and financial prospects, leading to investor damages. Investors are encouraged to contact Rosen Law Firm to learn more about joining the class action.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Gregory Hewett Sells 4,206 Shares of Acushnet Holdings Corp (GOLF)
feed.summary: Gregory Hewett, a Director at Acushnet Holdings Corp (GOLF), sold 4,206 shares of the company on March 4, 2026, reducing his total ownership to 37,017.47 shares. This transaction is part of a pattern over the past year with 12 insider sells and no insider buys at Acushnet. The stock is currently trading at $100, making it modestly overvalued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.35 and a P/E ratio of 26.11, higher than both the industry and company historical medians.

feed.title: Asbury Automotive Group stock hits 52-week low at 200.62 USD
feed.summary: Asbury Automotive Group (ABG) stock has reached a new 52-week low of $200.62, representing a 19.96% decrease over the past year. Despite its current P/E ratio of 8.26, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued relative to its Fair Value. The company recently reported slightly missed Q4 2025 earnings, sold an Indiana dealership, and saw a stock rally following a Supreme Court decision against tariffs.

feed.title: Walmart: Calling all entrepreneurs
feed.summary: Walmart has announced details for its Open Call 2026 program, an annual event giving US entrepreneurs the chance to pitch their products and supply chain solutions to Walmart and Sam's Club merchants. The program includes "Tour Stop" pitch meetings in various cities, leading up to Open Call 2026 in Bentonville in October for successful applicants. This initiative supports Walmart's commitment to US manufacturing, aiming to sell $350 billion in US-made goods by 2030 and support over 750,000 jobs.

feed.title: Krispy Kreme offering free doughnuts for Daylight Saving Time
feed.summary: Krispy Kreme is offering a special promotion to help customers with the dreaded "spring forward" for Daylight Saving Time. On Monday, customers can receive a free original glazed dozen with the purchase of any dozen. This offer is available at participating U.S. shops for in-store purchases, pickup, and delivery via the Krispy Kreme app or website using the code SPRING.

feed.title: Somnigroup, Formerly Tempur Sealy International, Inc., Posts Record Q4 2025 Results
feed.summary: Somnigroup International Inc. (formerly Tempur Sealy International) reported record fourth-quarter 2025 results, with net sales increasing 54.7% to $1,868.4 million, largely due to the acquisition of Mattress Firm. The company also saw a 20% increase in adjusted EPS and expects continued growth in 2026 despite macroeconomic uncertainties. Chairman and CEO Scott Thompson highlighted the company's strong competitive position through global scale, vertical integration, and omnichannel reach.

feed.title: Tokyo Lifestyle Secures HK$7.5 Million and JPY300 Million Insider Loans to Support Operations
feed.summary: Tokyo Lifestyle Limited, a subsidiary of Yoshitsu Co. Ltd. Sponsored ADR (TKLF), secured two insider loans totaling HK$7.5 million and JPY300 million to support operations. The loans, provided by controlling shareholder Mei Kanayama and a family-owned entity, are due in 2031 with bullet repayments. While these loans bolster long-term funding, they raise governance concerns due to increased reliance on insider financing.

feed.title: Starbucks to close store in Seattle's University District
feed.summary: Starbucks plans to close its University District store in Seattle on April 5th, following a three-month strike. This closure is part of a larger $1 billion turnaround plan the coffee company is implementing. The announcement was made by Egan Ward for the Puget Sound Business Journal.

feed.title: Bridgford: Overview of First Fiscal Quarter Earnings
feed.summary: Bridgford Foods Corp. reported a net loss of $843,000 for its fiscal first quarter, equating to a loss of 9 cents per share. The company generated $55.3 million in revenue during the quarter. Bridgford's stock declined by nearly 7% year-to-date and 30% over the past year, closing at $7.29 on Friday.

feed.title: Bridgford Foods 10-Q: Revenue $55.3M, Net Loss $0.84M
feed.summary: Bridgford Foods reported net sales of $55.3 million for the quarter, a 5.3% increase year-over-year attributed to price increases offsetting lower unit volumes. The company recorded a net loss of $0.843 million, an improvement compared to a $1.113 million loss in the previous year. Operational initiatives include focusing on private-label production, route consolidation, and enhanced vendor bidding, addressing challenges from rising costs in meat commodities, freight, healthcare, and temporary labor.

feed.title: Product in Motion: Gap Inc.’s Photo Studio Captures Clothing to Fit Your Lifestyle
feed.summary: Gap Inc.'s Photo Studio is essential for enhancing the online shopping experience across its brands (Gap, Banana Republic, Old Navy, Athleta). The studio focuses on capturing clothing from multiple angles and on models in lifelike poses to help customers evaluate fit, fabric, and styling. This centralized approach allows for shared learning and experimentation while maintaining distinct brand identities.

feed.title: Beyond Meat Disclosed Failure to Satisfy a Continued Listing Rule or Standard
feed.summary: Beyond Meat (BYND) received a Nasdaq deficiency letter on March 4, 2026, for failing to meet the $1.00 minimum bid price rule. The company has until August 31, 2026, to regain compliance, with potential options for an extension or a reverse stock split. Trading of BYND stock continues on the Nasdaq Global Select Market.

feed.title: SITE Centers eyes major redevelopments
feed.summary: SITE Centers Corp. is planning major redevelopments for key properties in its portfolio, exemplified by the $28 million renovation of West Bay Plaza in Westlake. This project involves replacing an older Kmart building with new retail spaces, including a Fresh Thyme Supermarket. The initiative highlights the company's strategy to enhance its properties.

feed.title: Medalist Diversified REIT Agrees to Sell Ashley Plaza for $16.6 Million to HPX
feed.summary: Medalist Diversified REIT (MDRR) has entered into an agreement to sell its Ashley Plaza retail property in Goldsboro, North Carolina, for $16.6 million to HPX Goldsboro Ashley Center. The buyer will provide $300,000 in earnest money, with the closing expected within 90 days. This transaction aims to recycle capital and optimize MDRR's portfolio.

feed.title: Advance Auto Parts opens a new store in Landover Hills, MD
feed.summary: Advance Auto Parts has opened a new store in Landover Hills, MD, with a strong commitment to employing local community members. The store offers free services like battery and diagnostic testing, wiper blade installation, and electrical system checks, alongside a Speed Perks loyalty program. In addition to in-store services, customers can utilize online shopping with same-day pickup or free delivery on orders over $35, and the team plans to engage with the community through car events and local job opportunities.

feed.title: Krispy Kreme 10-K: $1.52B Revenue, $(3.04) EPS
feed.summary: Krispy Kreme reported total net revenues of $1.52B, an 8.6% decrease from the previous year, and a net loss per diluted share of $(3.04), largely due to significant impairment charges. The company posted an operating loss of $(469.3) million and a net loss attributable to the company of $(515.8) million. Despite this, Krispy Kreme is pursuing a turnaround strategy through refranchising, reduced capital expenditures, and optimizing its hub operations.

feed.title: SA Asks: Which stocks are likely to be impacted by the Iran war? (UAL:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Seeking Alpha analysts Michael Boyd and Damir Tokic discuss the potential impact of a U.S.-Iran conflict on various stocks. Oil producers like Chord Energy, APA Corporation, and Kosmos Energy are highlighted as potential beneficiaries, while U.S. refiners like Valero and HF Sinclair could also see benefits. Conversely, the transportation sector (e.g., United Airlines), consumer staples (e.g., Walmart), and high-growth stocks are identified as vulnerable to rising oil prices.

feed.title: J Balvin remade Van Halen's 'Jump' as Coca-Cola's World Cup anthem. He says it was 'a puzzle'
feed.summary: J Balvin, along with Travis Barker, Amber Mark, and Steve Vai, has remade Van Halen's iconic song "Jump" as Coca-Cola's official anthem for the FIFA World Cup 2026. Balvin added a new Spanish verse to the song, giving it a Latin flavor and connecting it to the passion of fútbol. He expressed initial hesitation about touching such a classic but aimed to create a distinctive version, avoiding direct comparisons to the original.

feed.title: The Unexpected Grocery Chain That's One Of The Top Sushi Sellers In The US
feed.summary: Kroger has surprisingly emerged as the leading sushi seller in the U.S., reportedly selling over 40 million rolls annually. This success is attributed to its convenient placement within grocery stores, offering customers fresh sushi alongside their regular shopping. In an era of rising food costs and less dining out, Kroger capitalizes on the demand for prepared foods by providing restaurant-quality sushi at more accessible prices.

feed.title: Top Research Reports for Procter & Gamble, American Express & TJX
feed.summary: This article highlights new research reports from Zacks on 16 major stocks, including Procter & Gamble (PG), American Express (AXP), and TJX Companies (TJX), along with two micro-cap stocks, Genie Energy (GNE) and CompX International (CIX). The reports provide insights into each company's recent performance, market conditions, and future outlook, addressing both opportunities and challenges. It also mentions other featured reports on companies like Newmont Corp. (NEM) and Woodward, Inc. (WWD), among others.

feed.title: Dow rallies nearly 300 points on gains for Amazon.com Inc., Caterpillar stocks
feed.summary: This article reports that the Dow Jones Industrial Average rallied nearly 300 points, driven by significant gains in Amazon.com Inc. and Caterpillar Inc. stocks. The increase highlights positive market sentiment towards these major companies.

feed.title: Hostess, Peeps cupcakes hop onto shelves
feed.summary: Hostess Snacks has partnered with PEEPS to introduce new PEEPS Cupcakes for a limited time, celebrating the spring season. These cupcakes feature classic golden cake with marshmallow creme filling and bright yellow icing with the signature Hostess squiggle. They are available nationwide in an 8-count pack.

feed.title: O’Reilly Auto Announces $850 Million Senior Notes Offering
feed.summary: O’Reilly Automotive has announced an offering of $850 million in 5.100% senior notes due 2036. The net proceeds of approximately $841 million will be used to refinance existing debt due in 2026, reduce its commercial paper program, and for general corporate purposes including potential share repurchases and acquisitions. While analysts rate ORLY stock a "Buy" with a $109.00 price target, TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates it as "Neutral" due to strong profitability and cash generation being tempered by high balance-sheet leverage and rich valuation.

feed.title: Class I Briefs: CSX, NS
feed.summary: CSX celebrated the groundbreaking of a new Chick-fil-A Supply distribution center in Winter Haven, Florida, a $150 million investment expected to create 180 jobs and begin operations in 2027. Meanwhile, Norfolk Southern introduced its newest Landmark Series unit, the Altoona, featuring Automated Track Geometry Measurement Systems (ATGMS) which uses lasers and sensors to measure track geometry in real-time, enhancing safety and efficiency across its network. The article highlights both companies' commitment to improving their logistics and operational capabilities.

feed.title: Cal-Maine Foods Buys Creighton Brothers and Crystal Lake Assets for $128.5M
feed.summary: Cal-Maine Foods, Inc. (NASDAQ: CALM) announced the acquisition of shell egg, egg products, and prepared food assets of Creighton Brothers LLC, including Crystal Lake LLC, for approximately $128.5 million. The deal expands Cal-Maine's operations into new geographies and adds significant capacity, including 3.2 million laying hens and a feed mill, integrating the acquired businesses and their 177 employees into its existing structure.

feed.title: DA Davidson Sees Starbucks (SBUX) Turnaround Progress but Initiates at Neutral
feed.summary: DA Davidson has initiated coverage of Starbucks (SBUX) with a Neutral rating and a $97 price target, acknowledging progress in the company's turnaround efforts. However, the firm expresses uncertainty regarding the pace of same-store sales improvement and visibility on regaining historical flow-through margins. Separately, Starbucks is expanding its corporate presence by opening a new office in Nashville, Tennessee, to manage its North American supply chain and support growth in the central US, South, and Northeast.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Cuts GAP (NYSE:GAP) Price Target to $33.00
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has lowered its price target for GAP (NYSE:GAP) to $33.00 from $36.00, while maintaining an "overweight" rating. This adjustment follows GAP's mixed Q4 results, which saw shares plunge by 14.4% due to concerns over Athleta's performance, winter store closures, and margin pressures. Despite these issues, the company raised its FY2026 guidance and authorized a $1 billion share buyback, contributing to a "Moderate Buy" consensus among analysts.

feed.title: A Look Into Ulta Beauty Inc's Price Over Earnings
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty Inc. (NASDAQ: ULTA) shares are trading at $644.96 after a recent drop, though the stock has seen significant long-term growth. The article examines Ulta Beauty's Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio of 25.08, comparing it to the Specialty Retail industry's aggregate P/E of 199.37. It suggests that a lower P/E could indicate either undervaluation or an expectation of lower future performance, emphasizing the need for investors to consider other financial metrics and qualitative analyses.

feed.title: Hooker Furnishings Declares Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Hooker Furnishings Corporation (Nasdaq-GS: HOFT) announced that its board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.115 per share. The dividend is payable on March 31, 2026, to shareholders of record on March 16, 2026. The company is in its 102nd year of business, designing, marketing, and importing a wide range of furnishings for various markets, and also domestically manufactures premium residential custom leather, fabric-upholstered, and outdoor furniture.

feed.title: Hooker Furnishings to pay investors a $0.115-per-share dividend
feed.summary: Hooker Furnishings (Nasdaq: HOFT) declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.115 per share on March 5, 2026. The dividend is payable on March 31, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 16, 2026. The company, in its 102nd year, is a designer, marketer, and importer of various furniture types for residential, hospitality, and contract markets, with operations in the U.S. and Vietnam.

feed.title: European Wax Center Q3 Margin Resilience Tests Premium P/E Narrative
feed.summary: European Wax Center (EWCZ) reported Q3 FY 2025 revenue of US$54.2 million and EPS of US$0.09, maintaining a 5.4% net profit margin driven by an emphasis on recurring revenue. Despite a DCF fair value significantly higher than its current share price, the company faces scrutiny over its 22x P/E ratio, weak interest coverage, and only 1.6% earnings growth over the last year, which contrasts sharply with its five-year average. Investors are weighing the potential for improved marketing efficiency and visit frequency against concerns about balance sheet fragility and rising costs.

feed.title: EXEC: Journeys Stars Again in Genesco’s Q4 with Double-Digit Comp Encore
feed.summary: Genesco, Inc. exceeded expectations in Q4 fiscal 2026, primarily driven by its Journeys banner, which achieved a 12% same-store sales increase. The company plans to continue its "Footwear First" strategy, focusing on elevating Journeys' assortment and expanding its target demographic. While Schuh faced challenges due to promotional activity, Genesco aims to improve its profitability and overall store productivity across all segments in fiscal 2027.

feed.title: Etsy, Inc. Experiences Evaluation Revision Amidst Mixed Market Performance Indicators
feed.summary: Etsy, Inc. has undergone an evaluation revision due to fluctuating market conditions, with its stock currently priced at $56.63. Technical indicators show a mixed outlook, with both bearish and mildly bullish trends observed across different timeframes. While Etsy has shown short-term gains against the S&P 500, it has significantly lagged behind the index over longer periods, reflecting ongoing challenges in the E-Retail sector.

feed.title: KFC’s Saucy brand plans debut in Northeast Florida in March and April
feed.summary: KFC's new concept, Saucy, is set to open two locations in Northeast Florida in March and April, converting former PDQ restaurants. The first opening will be near St. Johns Town Center on March 23, followed by a Julington Creek location on April 13. Saucy, operated by Yum! Brands' KFC Corp., specializes in chicken tenders with a variety of sauces, sandwiches, desserts, and beverages, with plans for further expansion throughout the Southeast.

feed.title: Costco tops estimates; eyes 28 new openings, with 30-plus in ‘coming years’
feed.summary: Costco exceeded earnings and sales expectations for its holiday quarter, driven by strong consumer demand. The company plans 28 net new warehouse openings in the current fiscal year, with over 30 annually in the coming years, investing $6.5 billion in capital expenditures for expansion and improvements. CEO Ron Vachris also addressed the fluidity of tariffs and the company's commitment to returning any potential refunds to members through lower prices.

feed.title: Slam Dunks, Sweet Scoops and Shamrocks
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment in Las Vegas is gearing up for March with a variety of events, including college basketball viewing parties "Hoops on The Strip," St. Patrick's Day celebrations with green beer and festive food specials, and the launch of new cereal-inspired ice cream flavors at Salt & Straw. Additionally, Jennifer Lopez will continue her residency, and several restaurants across Caesars properties are introducing new menus and social hours.

feed.title: McDonald’s showcases employees in reality TV-inspired campaign
feed.summary: McDonald's is expanding its "1 in 8" campaign with a new "First Job Confessional" initiative inspired by reality TV. This campaign allows customers to share their first job stories in ordering kiosk "confessional booths" in various cities, with the chance to appear on the company's YouTube page. The effort reinforces McDonald's message that it is a popular first employer, equipping many with valuable career skills.

feed.title: W.W. Grainger (GWW) Reports 2025 Sales of $17.9B, Adjusted EPS of $39.48
feed.summary: W.W. Grainger Inc. (NYSE: GWW) reported strong full-year 2025 results with total sales of $17.9 billion, a 4.5% increase year-over-year, and adjusted diluted EPS of $39.48. The company also generated $2.0 billion in operating cash flow and returned $1.5 billion to shareholders. Despite some operating margin pressures, Grainger provided an optimistic outlook for 2026, projecting net sales between $18.7 billion and $19.1 billion and diluted EPS between $42.25 and $44.75.

feed.title: McDonald's (MCD) Target Price Raised to $385 by Tigress Financia
feed.summary: Tigress Financial has raised its price target for McDonald's (MCD) shares to $385 from $360, maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment reflects continued analyst confidence in McDonald's market position and profitability prospects, supported by a series of recent upward price target revisions from other firms. The company, the world's largest restaurant brand, is poised for potential growth according to Wall Street analysts and GuruFocus estimates.

feed.title: Brilliant Earth Shares Lower After B. Riley, KeyBanc Downgrades
feed.summary: Brilliant Earth Group, Inc. (BRLT) shares fell after B. Riley and KeyBanc downgraded the stock. B. Riley adjusted its price target to $1.50 from $3 and moved its rating to Neutral from Buy, while KeyBanc downgraded to Sector Weight from Overweight, citing margin risk. The downgrades followed the company's Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings report.

feed.title: e.l.f. Beauty Investigation Initiated: Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC Investigates the Officers and Directors of e.l.f. Beauty, Inc. - ELF
feed.summary: Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC (KSF) has initiated an investigation into e.l.f. Beauty, Inc. (ELF) following allegations from Muddy Waters Research of overstated revenue and concealed inventory issues. The investigation focuses on potential breaches of fiduciary duties by ELF's officers and directors after Muddy Waters reported material overstatements in revenue, inventory buildup, and misleading explanations for rising inventory levels. This probe comes after ELF's Q3 2025 results confirmed weaknesses identified by Muddy Waters.

feed.title: Instacart suit on hold as it takes wage law challenge to higher court
feed.summary: A federal judge has paused Instacart's lawsuit against New York City's new wage transparency and protection laws for delivery workers, pending the outcome of Instacart's appeal to a higher court. Instacart argues these local laws are preempted by federal and state regulations, specifically those for "motor carriers." While the appeal is underway, grocery delivery workers in NYC will continue to receive the new $21.44-per-hour minimum wage.

feed.title: AutoNation, Inc. Experiences Evaluation Revision Amidst Market Dynamics and Performance Metrics
feed.summary: AutoNation, Inc. has revised its evaluation due to changing market conditions. The company's stock is currently priced at $193.30, showing a 10.87% return over the past year, which underperforms the S&P 500. However, AutoNation has delivered a significant 125.27% return over the last five years, indicating resilience despite recent fluctuations.

feed.title: RAVE or PZZA: Which Pizza Chain Stock Should You Invest in Right Now?
feed.summary: This article compares two pizza chain stocks, Rave Restaurant Group (RAVE) and Papa John's International (PZZA), analyzing their business models, market landscape, stock performance, and valuation. It highlights RAVE's franchise-focused, asset-light model and consistent profitability, contrasting it with PZZA's larger international network and integrated supply chain. The author concludes that RAVE is currently the more attractive option due to its stability and reliable operating structure, despite PZZA's potential for a turnaround.

feed.title: Video: Essity GM Thiago Icassati discusses Edgewell feminine care acquisition, North America strategy
feed.summary: Essity's North America GM of personal care, Thiago Icassati, discusses the company's recent acquisition of Edgewell's feminine care brands (Carefree, Stayfree, Playtex, and o.b.). He elaborates on how this acquisition aligns with Essity's strategy to expand its presence in the hygiene CPG sector and touches upon the company's use of digital technology and global challenges. The acquisition was valued at $340 million and includes North American rights for Carefree, Stayfree, and o.b., along with global rights for Playtex feminine care.

feed.title: Key facts: Burlington Stores' Price Targets Rise; Expansion Strategy Set
feed.summary: Several financial firms have increased their price targets for Burlington Stores Inc. (BURL), with new targets ranging from $365.00 to $400.00, indicating stronger confidence in the company. Burlington Stores is also pursuing an expansion strategy, Burlington 2.0, aiming for $16 billion in sales and a 10% profit margin by 2028.

feed.title: Monro, Inc. Experiences Revision in Stock Evaluation Amid Market Volatility
feed.summary: Monro, Inc. has seen a revision in its stock evaluation, with its price now at $18.75 from a previous close of $19.76, reflecting market volatility and challenges in the retail sector. While technical indicators show mixed bullish and bearish signals, the company's performance has significantly lagged the S&P 500 across various time frames, particularly over three and five years. This underperformance highlights the ongoing difficulties Monro faces in a competitive market.

feed.title: Bloomin' Brands, Cracker Barrel, Jack in the Box, Dutch Bros, and Brinker International Stocks Trade Down, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several restaurant stocks, including Bloomin' Brands, Cracker Barrel, Jack in the Box, Dutch Bros, and Brinker International, traded down following a weaker-than-anticipated U.S. jobs report. The report, which indicated a cut of 92,000 jobs and an increase in the unemployment rate, suggested potential challenges for consumer discretionary spending, particularly affecting the dining industry. The article also touches on recent trade policy uncertainty that previously impacted Bloomin' Brands.

feed.title: Expert Outlook: PVH Through The Eyes Of 5 Analysts
feed.summary: Five analysts have recently provided ratings for PVH (NYSE: PVH), with an average 12-month price target of $89.4, representing an 11.66% decrease from previous targets. The company's financial performance shows modest revenue growth but struggles with net margin, return on equity, and return on assets compared to industry standards. However, PVH demonstrates a conservative financial approach with a debt-to-equity ratio below the industry average.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Neutral on Urban Outfitters (URBN)
feed.summary: Wells Fargo maintained an Equal Weight rating on Urban Outfitters (URBN) but reduced its target price to $75 from $80. The firm was positive on Urban Outfitter's Q4 FY 2026 earnings, noting a strong post-holiday recovery and optimistic FY 2027 guidance with projected high single-digit sales growth. Despite the potential for upside if tariffs decrease, Wells Fargo advises patience for investors due to the current market environment.

feed.title: KRISPY KREME® Helps Fans 'Spring Forward' with Sweet Deal: FREE Original Glazed® Dozen on Monday with Purchase of Any Dozen
feed.summary: Krispy Kreme is offering a sweet deal to help customers "spring forward" after Daylight Saving Time. On Monday, March 9, customers can receive a free Original Glazed dozen with the purchase of any dozen at participating U.S. shops, available in-shop, or for pickup/delivery via their app and website using promo code SPRING. This promotion aims to combat the grogginess from losing an hour of sleep.

feed.title: MercadoLibre (MELI) Reports 45% Q4 Revenue Growth, Full-Year Operating Income Up 22%
feed.summary: MercadoLibre (MELI) reported a 45% year-over-year increase in Q4 net revenues and a 22% rise in full-year operating income for 2025, despite an EPS miss. The company's growth was driven by investments in high-growth areas like shipping subsidies and credit card expansion, as well as significant gains in its commerce and fintech divisions across key Latin American markets. AI integration also played a crucial role, boosting advertising revenue and improving customer service efficiency.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark names Francesco Tinto as chief information officer
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark Corporation (NASDAQ:KMB) has appointed Francesco Tinto as its new Chief Information & Global Business Services Officer, effective Monday. Tinto, bringing over 30 years of technology leadership experience from companies like Advantage Solutions, Walgreens Boots Alliance, and Kraft Heinz, will lead the company's IT and Global Business Services organizations. Kimberly-Clark, with brands like Huggies and Kleenex, is currently valued at $34.8 billion and maintains a 4.94% dividend yield, having raised its dividend for 53 consecutive years.

feed.title: Kroger taps Mahadevan for new AI role
feed.summary: Kroger has appointed Milen Mahadevan, president and CEO of its 84.51 analytics division, to the newly created role of chief data and AI officer. Mahadevan will be responsible for embedding advanced AI into Kroger’s business processes and consolidating all data and AI teams under his leadership. This strategic move highlights Kroger's commitment to leveraging AI for improved customer experience and productivity across the company, especially as it operates under new leadership.

feed.title: Costco Talks Tariffs, Digital Growth
feed.summary: Costco reported strong Q2 sales, up 9.1% to $68.2 billion, with comparable-store sales rising 7.4% overall. The company is actively working to mitigate the impact of tariffs on customer prices and noted significant growth in its digitally enabled sales, which surged by 22.6%. Costco also lowered prices on several grocery items and plans to open 28 new warehouses in 2026, investing heavily in expansion and digital enhancements.

feed.title: Robinhood’s New $695 'Actual Platinum' Card Is an Insane Bet to Steal AmEx and Chase Customers
feed.summary: Robinhood, traditionally known for making stock trading accessible, has launched a new invitation-only premium credit card called "Actual Platinum" with a $695 annual fee. This card aims to compete with luxury offerings from American Express and JPMorgan Chase by providing rich rewards like 10% cash back on hotels and a $250 annual credit for autonomous-vehicle rides. The move signifies Robinhood's expansion into a full-service financial "super-app" to cater to its growing user base who have accumulated more wealth.

feed.title: Walmart Fails in Bid to Nix Website Patent Infringement Suit
feed.summary: A US District Court judge denied Walmart Inc.'s bid to dismiss a patent infringement lawsuit filed by Adaptive Avenue Associates Inc. The lawsuit alleges that Walmart infringed on patents related to systems for creating website presentations and navigating multiple websites. The judge stated that plausible factual disputes exist, requiring Walmart to continue defending against the claims.

feed.title: Kroger is closing stores: See the updated list that shows shuttered locations across the country
feed.summary: Kroger is currently in the process of closing about 60 stores as part of a "footprint optimization plan" announced last year. A Fast Company review indicates that 33 stores have already closed, with at least three more confirmed for this year, impacting various Kroger-owned banners. The company has not publicly released a list of affected locations, making it difficult to ascertain the full scope and impact on jobs.

feed.title: (CAL) Price Dynamics and Execution-Aware Positioning
feed.summary: This article analyzes Caleres Inc. (NYSE: CAL) dynamics, indicating a weak sentiment across all horizons that supports a short bias. It outlines distinct AI-generated trading strategies including long, momentum breakout, and short positions with specific entry, target, and stop-loss levels. The analysis emphasizes an exceptional risk-reward setup targeting a 17.6% gain versus a 0.3% risk.

feed.title: Home Depot vs. Lowe’s: One-stop convenience and better prices crown the winner
feed.summary: This article compares Home Depot and Lowe's, two major home improvement retailers, to determine which offers better convenience and pricing. While both companies use "price-scraper" tech to keep common item prices similar, Lowe’s is highlighted for offering more frequent "Deals of the Day," an automatic 5% discount with its consumer credit card, better bundle deals on appliances, and a free rewards program for everyday shoppers. Lowe's is also noted for being generally cleaner and more appealing to homeowners and DIYers, featuring more "finish" products and often ranking higher in consumer satisfaction.

feed.title: Building on Strong 2025 Growth, Ross Plans to Add 110 Stores in 2026
feed.summary: Ross Stores reported an 8% increase in net sales for FY 2025, reaching $22.8 billion, driven by value-seeking consumers and a 5% rise in comparable sales. Building on this success, the discount apparel retailer plans to open 110 new stores in FY 2026, representing a 5% growth rate. This expansion includes 85 new Ross stores and 25 new dd’s Discounts locations, aiming for long-term growth to 2,900 and 700 stores respectively.

feed.title: 3 Dividend King Stocks That Yield Over 4% and Have Big Upside
feed.summary: This article identifies three Dividend King stocks—Kimberly-Clark (KMB), Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT), and Stanley Black & Decker (SWK)—that offer dividend yields over 4% and significant upside potential. These companies are highlighted as stable investment options as growth stocks cool and interest rates decline, benefiting from their long histories of consistent dividend increases. The analysis provides specific reasons for optimism for each stock, including Kenvue acquisition synergies for KMB, strong leasing performance for FRT, and recovery potential for SWK.

feed.title: How Record 2025 Volumes And Mexico Tax Headwinds At Coca-Cola FEMSA (KOF) Have Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: Coca-Cola FEMSA reported mixed 2025 results with record volumes but modest profit growth, alongside the looming challenge of increased excise taxes in Mexico for 2026. This situation is shifting the investment focus from pure volume growth to margin and mix management, despite the company's long-term revenue and earnings projections. Investors are advised to consider how these new tax headwinds and analyst perspectives might impact pricing power and the stock's valuation.

feed.title: CVS teams up with Google Cloud to launch AI health platform
feed.summary: CVS Health is partnering with Google Cloud to introduce Health100, an AI-powered health platform. This HIPAA-compliant platform is designed to consolidate data from various sources including insurers, providers, pharmacies, and labs. The launch of Health100 is anticipated later this year.

feed.title: Theres a Brand-New Five Below Store That Opened in New Hampshire
feed.summary: Five Below has opened its ninth store in New Hampshire, located in Concord at 80 Storrs Street, Unit 160 within the Capital Shopping Center. The new store opened on Friday, March 6, offering a wide range of affordable products, most priced between $1 and $5. This expansion continues Five Below's growth in New England, adding another shopping option for residents.

feed.title: Shake Shack, Wingstop, The Cheesecake Factory, and Sweetgreen Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Shake Shack, Wingstop, The Cheesecake Factory, and Sweetgreen plummeted after a weaker-than-anticipated U.S. jobs report signaled potential challenges for consumer spending, particularly impacting the restaurant industry. The Labor Department reported a cut of 92,000 jobs and an unemployment rate increase to 4.4%, leading to concerns about reduced discretionary spending. This news exacerbates existing issues in the dining sector, with many full-service restaurants facing closure risks and unprofitability.

feed.title: Crocs' Margins Under Pressure: Temporary Dip or Structural Shift?
feed.summary: Crocs' margins have recently declined, attributed mainly to tariff-related cost pressures and strategic adjustments within its HEYDUDE brand. Despite a 50 basis point decrease in enterprise adjusted gross margin in 2025, the core Crocs brand maintains strong resilience. Management anticipates modest margin improvement through cost savings and supply-chain optimization, suggesting the current pressure is temporary rather than a fundamental flaw.

feed.title: Kroger names chief data and AI officer
feed.summary: Kroger has appointed Milen Mahadevan as its first chief data and AI officer, emphasizing the grocer's commitment to accelerating AI across its operations. Mahadevan, who also leads Kroger's retail analytics subsidiary 84.51˚, will focus on developing "agentic" AI capabilities to enhance both customer experience and business productivity. This move aligns with broader retail trends where companies like Target and Albertsons are also increasing AI investments to boost efficiency and improve customer interactions.

feed.title: PDC Brands, Church & Dwight, Mars, Lowe's Execs to Discuss Intelligent Supply Chains at Analytics Unite
feed.summary: Executives from PDC Brands, Church & Dwight, Mars, and Lowe's will participate in a panel discussion at Analytics Unite 2026 to discuss intelligent supply chains. They will explore how autonomous and AI-enabled technologies can reduce risk, improve efficiency, and foster growth amid market volatility. The event, taking place in Chicago from April 7-9, will focus on leveraging advanced analytics to manage fluctuating demand and global disruptions.

feed.title: 3 Fast Food Stocks to Buy Right Now
feed.summary: This article highlights three fast-food stocks—McDonald's (MCD), Yum! Brands (YUM), and Wingstop (WING)—as attractive investment opportunities. It references recent social media buzz around McDonald's CEO, but focuses on the companies' strong financial models, growth potential, and analyst upgrades, amidst a growing fast-food market driven by consumer demand and urbanization.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty: Quarterly Preview And The Case For A Premium (NASDAQ:ULTA)
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty, Inc. (ULTA) is reaffirmed as a Buy with a raised price target of $800, based on robust fundamentals and premium business quality. The company's differentiated model, large loyalty base, and data-driven customer acquisition strategies provide a long-term competitive edge. Strategic moves like expanding internationally and launching high-margin programs support a quality premium for ULTA stock despite its current high valuation multiples.

feed.title: Chipotle, Darden, Krispy Kreme, and Kura Sushi Stocks Trade Down, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several restaurant stocks, including Chipotle, Darden, Krispy Kreme, and Kura Sushi, experienced significant drops today following a weaker-than-anticipated U.S. jobs report, which signaled potential challenges for consumer spending. The Labor Department reported an unexpected cut of 92,000 jobs and an increase in the unemployment rate, particularly impacting the restaurant and bar industry. This news compounds existing concerns about the sector's profitability and risk of closures, with Kura Sushi seeing an especially volatile decrease.

feed.title: Here's Why You Should Add EAT Stock to Your Portfolio Right Now
feed.summary: Brinker International (EAT) shows strong investment potential due to its consistent same-store sales growth, particularly at Chili's, and a focus on menu innovation. The company's effective marketing and remodeling initiatives have driven guest traffic and engagement, contributing to its favorable Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) and high return on equity. Despite inflation concerns, strategic pricing and menu enhancements are supporting continued growth.

feed.title: BBWI Deadline: BBWI Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Bath & Body Works, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: The Rosen Law Firm is reminding investors who purchased Bath & Body Works (BBWI) securities between June 4, 2024, and November 19, 2025, that the lead plaintiff deadline for a securities fraud lawsuit is March 16, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Bath & Body Works made materially false and misleading statements about its growth strategy, which failed to grow its customer base and deliver expected sales. Investors are encouraged to contact the law firm to join the class action and potentially seek compensation.

feed.title: Chipotle, Darden, Krispy Kreme, and Kura Sushi Stocks Trade Down, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Chipotle, Darden, Krispy Kreme, and Kura Sushi declined following a weaker-than-anticipated U.S. jobs report, which indicated potential challenges for consumer spending, especially in the restaurant and bar industry. The report showed a loss of 92,000 jobs last month and an increase in the unemployment rate, raising concerns about reduced discretionary spending. Kura Sushi, already volatile, was significantly impacted, with its stock falling 10% after previous drops related to global economic concerns.

feed.title: Bloomin' Brands, Cracker Barrel, Jack in the Box, Dutch Bros, and Brinker International Stocks Trade Down, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several restaurant stocks including Bloomin' Brands, Cracker Barrel, Jack in the Box, Dutch Bros, and Brinker International experienced a downturn after a weaker-than-anticipated U.S. jobs report signaled potential challenges for consumer spending. The Labor Department reported a loss of 92,000 jobs, particularly impacting the restaurant and bar industry, leading to concerns about reduced discretionary spending. This news compounds existing sector worries, with a recent analysis indicating that 9% of full-service restaurants are at risk of closure in 2026.

feed.title: BJ's Wholesale Club Stock Pulls Back, Analyst Says Buy The Dip
feed.summary: D.A. Davidson analyst Michael Baker reiterated a Buy rating on BJ's Wholesale Club (NYSE: BJ) stock, seeing its recent pullback as a buying opportunity despite near-term concerns. The firm raised its price target to $114 due to strong membership growth, improving comparable sales, and significant whitespace for expansion. Although there was a decline in merchandise margins and cautious Q1/2026 EPS guidance, Baker expects continued strong execution from BJ's.

feed.title: American Rebel Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:AREB) And American Rebel Light Beer Roar into Gainesville as NHRA’s 75th Anniversary Season Opens at the 2026 Amalie Motor Oil NHRA Gatornationals as The Official Beer Sponsor
feed.summary: American Rebel Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:AREB) and American Rebel Light Beer are the official beer sponsor for the 2026 Amalie Motor Oil NHRA Gatornationals in Gainesville, marking the 75th anniversary season of NHRA. The event will feature an American Rebel Beer Garden, live music from CEO Andy Ross, and support for Tony Stewart Racing and Pro Stock Motorcycle rider John Hall. This move expands American Rebel's presence in motorsports, emphasizing patriotism, freedom, and high-quality light beer.

feed.title: Ex-Dividend Reminder: Kontoor Brands, CRA International and Stanley Black & Decker
feed.summary: Kontoor Brands (KTB), CRA International (CRAI), and Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) are set to trade ex-dividend on March 10, 2026. This means investors need to own shares before this date to receive their respective quarterly dividends of $0.53, $0.57, and $0.83. The article also provides historical dividend charts and current estimated annualized yields for each company.

feed.title: Whitestone Hires Bank Of America To Handle Possible Acquisition
feed.summary: Whitestone REIT has hired Bank of America to oversee potential acquisition proceedings after receiving interest from major real estate players like Blackstone and TPG. The Houston-based shopping center REIT has faced past pressure from stakeholders regarding its leadership and finances, including turned down bids and proxy contests. There is no guarantee a sale will materialize, but the move signals further exploration of a change in ownership.

feed.title: Socket Mobile announces iPhone 17e scanner compatibility
feed.summary: Socket Mobile Inc. announced that its XtremeScan, DuraSled, and DuraScan Terminal scanning products are now compatible with Apple’s new iPhone 17e. The company, facing financial challenges including a ~21% stock decline over the past year and decreasing revenue, aims to support businesses deploying iOS-based data capture systems in industrial and commercial settings. The announcement highlights Socket Mobile's continued alignment with Apple and its focus on providing durable and reliable industrial scanning solutions through its CaptureSDK software.

feed.title: Grocery Outlet closing 5 stores in South Jersey, see SJ stores impacted
feed.summary: Grocery Outlet announced it will close 36 "underperforming stores" across the country, including five in South Jersey, due to expanding too quickly. CEO Jason Potter stated that this move aims to improve adjusted earnings and implement a more "disciplined approach" to future store openings and operations. Despite the closures, the company plans to open 30-33 new stores in 2026 and refresh 150 existing ones.

feed.title: Clarus Corporation Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Clarus Corporation has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025, showing a decrease in sales but an improvement in adjusted net income. The company is focusing on a simplification strategy, refining its organizational structure, product portfolio, and go-to-market approach to drive profitable growth. For fiscal year 2026, Clarus anticipates sales between $255 million and $265 million and adjusted EBITDA between $9 million and $11 million.

feed.title: Assessing Cinemark Holdings (CNK) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum And Three Year Returns
feed.summary: Cinemark Holdings (CNK) has shown strong short-term and long-term share price momentum, with a 123.7% three-year total shareholder return. Despite this performance and positive growth, analysts suggest the stock is still undervalued by 11.2% at $28.25 versus a narrative fair value of $31.82. However, this valuation is dependent on factors like steady film slate and attendance, with potential risks from box office volatility and streaming trends.

feed.title: Once Upon a Farm debuts refrigerated meat pouches for babies
feed.summary: Once Upon a Farm is expanding its product line with new organic offerings for babies and children, including refrigerated meat pouches, functional smoothies, and whole-grain bars. The meat pouches come in chicken, beef, turkey, bone broth, black bean, and chickpea varieties, each providing at least 4 grams of protein. The new products are set to be available at select retailers and on the company's website starting in April.

feed.title: Genesco Inc. reports strong Q4, with ‘exceptional’ performance at Journeys
feed.summary: Genesco Inc. announced strong fourth-quarter results, driven by exceptional performance from its Journeys brand, marking its sixth consecutive quarter of positive comparable sales growth. The company exceeded analyst expectations for both earnings and sales, with net income rising to $47.6 million and net sales reaching $799.9 million. Genesco anticipates continued comparable sales growth in the current fiscal year, supported by strategic initiatives and ongoing strength in Journeys, despite expected offsets from Schuh.

feed.title: The Chefs' Warehouse Experiences Revision in Stock Evaluation Amid Market Dynamics
feed.summary: The Chefs' Warehouse, Inc. has seen a revision in its stock evaluation due to changing market conditions, with its stock currently priced at $63.22. Despite recent underperformance against the S&P 500, the company has demonstrated resilience with positive returns over longer timeframes, including an 82.09% return over three years. Technical indicators show mixed signals, with weekly bullish MACD and Bollinger Bands, but some monthly bearish trends.

feed.title: Investors Are Actively Seeking Information on Walmart Inc. (WMT): Essential Details You Should Be Aware Of
feed.summary: This article examines Walmart's (WMT) recent stock performance and future outlook, highlighting its status as one of the most searched stocks on Zacks.com. It details recent stock declines, updates on earnings projections and revenue growth expectations, and recent quarterly results. The article also touches upon stock valuation using Zacks' metrics, concluding with Walmart holding a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) due to its neutral short-term outlook.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Simon Property Group (SPG) After A 21% One-Year Rally?
feed.summary: Simon Property Group (SPG) has seen a 21% rally over the past year, prompting an evaluation of its current valuation. According to Simply Wall St's analysis, SPG appears undervalued based on five out of six valuation tests, particularly its Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) and Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio, suggesting a market price below its estimated fair value. The article encourages investors to consider these findings and explore community insights to form their own 'Narrative' about the company's future prospects.

feed.title: Copart, Inc. Experiences Evaluation Revision Amid Mixed Market Signals and Performance Trends
feed.summary: Copart, Inc. has undergone a recent evaluation revision due to challenging market conditions, with its stock priced at $38.02. The company has significantly underperformed the S&P 500 over the past year and decade, with mixed signals from technical indicators pointing towards volatility and bearish sentiment. This highlights the ongoing struggles Copart faces within the retail sector.

feed.title: Ready for Costco in Port St. Lucie? Here's where it would be built
feed.summary: Port St. Lucie is moving closer to getting a Costco store, with the city's Government Finance Corp. set to decide on selling 23 acres in Southern Grove to Costco Wholesale Corp. for a new retail store and gas station. This development follows years of speculation about Costco's arrival in the area. The proposed store would be 170,000 square feet.

feed.title: Williams-Sonoma’s GreenRow opens flagship store in SoHo
feed.summary: Williams-Sonoma’s sustainably-focused home brand, GreenRow, has launched its first brick-and-mortar flagship store in New York City's SoHo neighborhood. The store, located at 47 Howard Street, is designed to offer an immersive, home-like experience showcasing GreenRow's sustainable furnishings across various categories. A portion of sales from the grand opening weekend will benefit the New York Botanical Garden, emphasizing the brand's commitment to environmental and social initiatives.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Simon Property Group (SPG) After A 21% One-Year Rally?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Simon Property Group (SPG) after its 21% one-year rally, assessing its current valuation. Using Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) and Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio analyses, Simply Wall St suggests that SPG appears undervalued. The DCF model indicates a 27.4% discount, while its P/E ratio of 14.13x is significantly lower than the industry average and Simply Wall St's proprietary "Fair Ratio."

feed.title: Jonas Brothers returning to MGM Resorts on 20th anniversary
feed.summary: The Jonas Brothers are returning to Dolby Live at Park MGM for four dates in May as part of their "Greetings From Your Hometown" tour, celebrating their 20-year career. The tour will feature music from all eras, including their solo projects. The group also has a culinary presence in Las Vegas with Nellie's Southern Kitchen at MGM Grand.

feed.title: 2 furniture stores, Dollar Tree coming to Richmond
feed.summary: Richmond, Indiana is set to welcome new businesses including two furniture stores, Richmond Furniture East and Ashley Furniture, and a new Dollar Tree location. While these stores are opening, the Italian fast-food restaurant Fazoli's recently closed permanently in the area. The new businesses are expected to provide more shopping options for residents.

feed.title: Williams-Sonoma (NYSE:WSM) Price Target Raised to $208.00
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Williams-Sonoma (NYSE:WSM) to $208.00 from $188.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating, which implies an 11.06% potential upside. The company currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with an average price target of $203.22. Additionally, recent insider activity shows CEO Laura Alber sold 35,000 shares for over $7.2 million.

feed.title: Oppenheimer Trims Target Price on CarGurus (CARG) to $38
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has lowered its target price for CarGurus (CARG) to $38 from $40, while maintaining an Outperform rating. This adjustment is attributed to the impact of the AI disruption narrative on tech and software valuation multiples. Despite the cut, Oppenheimer remains optimistic about CARG due to its proprietary used car valuation data, strong dealer relationships, and new product offerings that are expected to drive high incremental margins and durable revenue growth.

feed.title: RB Global Expands Ag Footprint with BigIron Acquisition
feed.summary: RB Global, formerly Ritchie Bros. Auctioneers, is set to acquire BigIron Auctions to expand its footprint in the U.S. agricultural sector. The acquisition aims to provide farmers with more tools for buying and selling equipment while allowing BigIron to maintain its leadership and standalone brand. This merger reflects a broader consolidation trend in the agricultural auction industry, driven by an increasing need for advanced digital marketing tools.

feed.title: Is Brixmor Property Group (BRX) Now Attractively Priced After Strong Recent Share Gains?
feed.summary: Brixmor Property Group (BRX) has shown strong recent share gains, with returns of 18.2% over the past year. A Discounted Cash Flow analysis suggests the stock is currently undervalued by 15.0%, with an estimated intrinsic value of $35.55 per share compared to its recent price of $30.21. Additionally, Brixmor's current P/E ratio of 24.0x is below both the industry average and Simply Wall St's proprietary "Fair Ratio" of 29.6x, further indicating it might be attractively priced.

feed.title: Krispy Kreme $1.6 Million Data Breach Deal Gets First Court Nod
feed.summary: Krispy Kreme Inc. has received initial court approval for a $1.6 million settlement in a class action lawsuit stemming from a 2024 data breach. The breach exposed the personal information of nearly 162,000 employees. Class members may claim up to $3,500 for documented losses or an alternative cash payment of approximately $75.

feed.title: Dine Brands targets non-traditional spaces for dual-branded concept
feed.summary: Dine Brands is expanding its dual-branded Applebee’s/IHOP concept into non-traditional locations such as airports, universities, and hospitals, aiming to surpass its initial goal of 900 units. These locations are ideal due to their 24/7 populations and demand for all-day dining options. The company emphasizes optimized menus, efficient kitchen layouts, and catering to both morning and evening dayparts to maximize revenue, already seeing 1.5 to 2.5 times greater revenue than single-branded restaurants.

feed.title: Destination Fees Continue to Rise in 2026
feed.summary: Destination fees for new vehicles continue to sharply rise in 2026, making up a significant portion of a car's MSRP. Automakers attribute these increases to external factors like tariffs and supply chain issues, but some analysts suggest profit-taking. Domestic brands show the most substantial increases, particularly for large trucks and SUVs, with some destination charges exceeding 5% of the vehicle's total cost.

feed.title: RAVE vs. PZZA: Which Pizza Restaurant Stock Is the Better Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article compares Rave Restaurant Group (RAVE) and Papa John's International (PZZA), examining their business models, stock performance, and valuation. It concludes that RAVE, with its franchise-heavy structure and stable operations, appears to be the better buy due to more predictable returns and recent stronger market performance, despite PZZA's lower valuation presenting potential turnaround upside.

feed.title: DICK'S Q4 Earnings on the Cards: Is It Poised to Beat Expectations?
feed.summary: DICK'S Sporting Goods Inc. (DKS) is set to release its Q4 fiscal 2025 earnings on March 12, with expectations of a 56.7% year-over-year sales increase, reaching $6.1 billion, despite a projected 5.3% dip in earnings per share to $3.43. The company's performance is anticipated to be bolstered by strong strategic efforts, brand strength, market share gains, and a robust digital ecosystem. However, macroeconomic uncertainties, tariff-related costs, and increased SG&A expenses due to strategic investments may weigh on profitability, and the Zacks model does not conclusively predict an earnings beat.

feed.title: California's Beyond Meat is dropping the meat
feed.summary: Beyond Meat, the plant-based meat company, is rebranding to "Beyond The Plant Protein Co." and dropping "meat" from its name and packaging. This strategic shift reflects a greater focus on protein products beyond just meat alternatives, such as sparkling protein drinks and protein bars. The change comes amidst a 26% drop in plant-based meat sales over the past two years and a 14% revenue decline for Beyond Meat, as consumer concerns about added sugars and sodium affect the market.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider US Foods Holding (USFD) After Strong Multi Year Rally?
feed.summary: US Foods Holding (USFD) has seen significant share price appreciation over recent years. While some investors might be concerned about its valuation after such a strong rally, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock could be undervalued by 40.9%. Furthermore, its P/E ratio, while close to its peers, is considered undervalued when compared to Simply Wall St's proprietary "Fair Ratio."

feed.title: Haidilao's International Arm Super Hi Set To Post Strong Annual Profit, But Competition Intensifies
feed.summary: Haidilao's international arm, Super Hi, is projected to achieve strong annual profits, with revenues from restaurants outside China expected to rise by nearly 8% in 2025 and profits to jump 56%. However, this growth is somewhat inflated by currency gains, and the company is facing increased competition in overseas markets, leading to crimped margins due to higher labor and operating costs. Super Hi is strategically absorbing some profitability pressure to maintain market share and is focusing on enhancing single-store efficiency and localized offerings as competition intensifies.

feed.title: Gap revival crystallizes as sales jump 8% in Q4
feed.summary: Gap Inc. reported a Q4 net sales increase of 2% year over year, reaching $4.2 billion, driven by strong performances from Old Navy, namesake Gap, and Banana Republic. The Gap brand, in particular, saw an 8% sales jump and its eighth consecutive quarter of comparable sales increases, signaling a successful strategic pivot. However, the activewear brand Athleta continued to struggle with an 11% sales decline, and overall margins and profits were negatively impacted by tariffs and extensive store closures due to extreme weather.

feed.title: Must Read: Victoria Beckham's Business Is Growing, Gap Inc. Reports Q4 2025 Sales Gain
feed.summary: Victoria Beckham's fashion and beauty business has seen significant growth in 2025, with sales up 19% and core operating profit quadrupling, leading to plans for new stores in New York and Paris. Concurrently, Gap Inc. reported a 2% increase in net sales for both Q4 and the full fiscal year 2025, driven by a 4% rise in online sales. Other fashion industry news includes efforts by the fragrance industry to preserve endangered scents, Asics' acquisition of race-registration platforms, a trend of hypermasculinity at recent fashion weeks, and Stella McCartney receiving the Legion of Honor.

feed.title: Gap Q4 Earnings & Sales Meet Estimates, Revenues Rise Y/Y
feed.summary: Gap Inc. (GAP) reported fiscal Q4 2025 earnings and sales that met Zacks Consensus Estimates, with revenues increasing year-over-year due to strong brand momentum across Old Navy, Gap, and Banana Republic. Despite a decline in bottom-line earnings, comparable sales rose 3% and online sales grew 5%. The company also provided an outlook for Q1 and full fiscal year 2026, anticipating net sales growth and an increase in capital expenditures for store upgrades and technology.
