feed.title: Gap stock sinks after earnings. The real story may be what happened to 800 stores
feed.summary: Gap Inc. shares dropped over 12% following its fourth-quarter earnings report, which met revenue expectations but slightly missed EPS predictions. The company's performance was impacted by the temporary closure of 800 stores due to severe winter storms. While the Gap, Banana Republic, and Old Navy brands saw comparable sales increases, Athleta experienced a significant 10% decline.

feed.title: BioRestorative Therapies, Inc. to Participate in the Be+Well Beauty and Wellness Show in New York City, March 8-10, 2026
feed.summary: BioRestorative Therapies, Inc. announced its participation in the Be+Well Beauty and Wellness Show in New York City from March 8-10, 2026. The company aims to expand commercialization and distribution for its BioCosmeceutical platform, which applies regenerative biologics technology to aesthetics and longevity markets. This engagement seeks to connect with industry partners, distributors, and practitioners to leverage its biotechnology foundation in the rapidly growing regenerative skincare sector.

feed.title: Black Rock Coffee Bar (BRCB) Thin FY 2025 Profit Margins Test Bullish Growth Narratives
feed.summary: Black Rock Coffee Bar (BRCB) recently closed FY 2025 with strong revenue growth and an expanding store count, achieving Q4 revenue of US$53.6 million. Despite increasing sales and narrowing net losses over the past two years, profitability remains thin, raising questions about whether the company can convert its top-line momentum into consistent positive earnings. Analysts project significant future growth, but the current valuation metrics, including a P/S ratio and DCF fair value, suggest investors are paying a premium based on future potential rather than current profits.

feed.title: Campbell Soup Slashes 200 Jobs at Paris Plant in Sauce Shift Shakeup
feed.summary: Campbell Soup Co. is cutting over 200 jobs at its Paris, Texas, plant as part of a multi-year retooling to transition the facility from soup production to a flagship sauce plant. This move follows an earlier announcement in July 2024 to wind down soup operations and convert the site for Prego and Pace sauces, affecting a significant portion of the workforce. Local officials and workforce agencies are expected to provide transition and retraining support for the affected employees.

feed.title: Jack in the Box is back in Florida. Here's where to find it
feed.summary: Jack in the Box is returning to Florida after a 46-year absence, with its first new location recently opened in Longwood, and more planned for Orlando and Tallahassee. The fast-food chain is celebrating its 75th anniversary and is known for its diverse menu, all-day breakfast, and pioneering the drive-thru intercom system. The expansion is part of the company's strategic growth plan for the Southeast.

feed.title: A Look At Boot Barn Holdings (BOOT) Valuation After Recent Share Price Swings
feed.summary: Boot Barn Holdings (BOOT) is currently trading at $184.29, below an average analyst target of $238.29, and is considered 18.9% undervalued with a narrative fair value of $227.14 based on robust store expansion and strong customer acquisition. However, its P/E ratio of 25.6x is higher than the US Specialty Retail average of 19.9x, suggesting the market may already be pricing in future growth. The valuation hinges on continued store expansion and demand for western and workwear.

feed.title: IPW Plunges 80.5% in 3 Months: Should You Still Hold the Stock or Sell?
feed.summary: iPower Inc. (IPW) has seen its stock plunge 80.5% in the last three months, significantly underperforming the industry. The company is undergoing a strategic restructuring of its supply chain, shifting to U.S.-based sourcing, and exploring new ventures in crypto infrastructure hardware, which has caused short-term operational disruptions and revenue declines. While the current valuation appears discounted, presenting potential for value-oriented investors if operations stabilize, existing shareholders are advised to proceed cautiously and monitor execution risks.

feed.title: Williams-Sonoma unveils first store for its GreenRow brand
feed.summary: Williams-Sonoma Inc. has opened the first physical store for its sustainable home furnishings brand, GreenRow, in New York City's SoHo neighborhood. Launched in 2023, GreenRow focuses on innovative, sustainable manufacturing practices and low-impact materials. The SoHo store is designed to resemble a lived-in home, showcasing GreenRow's full product range and a curated selection of art and vintage pieces, aiming to offer customers an immersive experience.

feed.title: Reborn Coffee appoints Jung Jae Lim as Co-CEO
feed.summary: Reborn Coffee (REBN) has announced the appointment of Jung Jae Lim as Co-Chief Executive Officer, effective March 3, 2026. In this new role, Lim will focus on improving the company's operational foundation by leading initiatives in logistics, transportation, and supply chain management. This appointment was first published by TheFly.

feed.title: General Mills removes certified colors from all school meal items
feed.summary: General Mills has successfully removed all certified colors from its entire portfolio of K-12 school meal items, achieving this goal ahead of its summer 2026 commitment. This move, which involved reformulating Lucky Charms 25% less sugar cereal to reach 100% compliance, aligns with the company's broader objective to remove certified colors from its US cereal portfolio by summer 2026 and its full US retail portfolio by the end of 2027, spurred by the FDA's intention to phase out petroleum-based synthetic food dyes.

feed.title: Darden Restaurants Inc (DRI) Trading Down 3.46% on Mar 6
feed.summary: Darden Restaurants Inc (DRI) saw its shares trade down 3.46% on March 6, reaching an intraday low of $198.10 before closing at $199.07. Wall Street analysts forecast an average target price of $221.33 for DRI, suggesting an 11.18% upside, with a consensus "Outperform" rating. GuruFocus estimates the stock's fair value (GF Value) at $213.76, implying a 7.38% upside from the current price.

feed.title: Is Clorox's International Expansion Key to Its Long-Term Success?
feed.summary: Clorox is increasingly focusing on international expansion, especially in high-growth emerging markets, to drive long-term success as domestic growth slows. While this strategy offers new growth avenues and reduces reliance on the U.S. market, it also presents challenges like currency volatility, regulatory complexities, and competitive pressures. The company aims to balance brand recognition with pricing power, and invest in localized product innovation to succeed abroad.

feed.title: Why Did Chipotle Mexican Grill Stock Drop Today?
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill's stock dropped 3.6% despite a "buy" rating initiation by DA Davidson analyst Matt Curtis. The main reason for investor hesitation appears to be the lack of detailed initiatives supporting the projected sales rebound and the stock's high valuation of 32 times trailing earnings, which many believe is too expensive for a company expecting "mid-single digit" growth.

feed.title: Chipotle Opens a Record 334 Restaurants in 2025: Here’s What Comes Next
feed.summary: Chipotle opened a record 334 new restaurants in 2025 and is implementing a "Recipe for Growth" strategy to improve comparable sales and margins, targeting $4 million average unit volumes (AUVs) and 30% restaurant-level margins. Despite a projected near-term margin trough in 2026, analysts see significant upside, with TIKR's mid-case valuation model suggesting a 102.3% total return by 2030 based on the success of its equipment rollout and operational improvements. The company's share price is currently trading 36% below its 52-week high, presenting a "transaction recovery play" for investors.

feed.title: Coca-Cola Stock Dips 1.4% to $77 as Shares Pull Back from Recent Highs Amid Consumer Caution
feed.summary: Shares of The Coca-Cola Company (KO) pulled back by 0.3% to 1.4% to around $77 on Friday, March 6, 2026, retreating from February's all-time highs near $82. This decline is attributed to macro headwinds like higher energy costs and cautious consumer spending, despite the company's strong Q4 and full-year 2025 results and its consistent dividend increases. Analysts largely maintain a "Buy" rating, citing Coca-Cola's defensive profile and brand strength, seeing the current dip as a potential entry point for long-term investors.

feed.title: Rail News - CSX to serve future Chick-fil-A Supply site in Florida. For Railroad Career Professionals
feed.summary: CSX will provide rail service to a new Chick-fil-A Supply distribution center in Winter Haven, Florida. This 244,000-square-foot facility represents a $150 million investment and is expected to create 180 jobs, supporting statewide distribution for Chick-fil-A restaurants. Operations are set to begin in 2027, further establishing central Florida as a logistics hub.

feed.title: Burlington Stores Analysts Raise Their Forecasts After Better-Than-Expected Q4 Earnings
feed.summary: Burlington Stores (NYSE: BURL) reported strong fourth-quarter financial results, exceeding revenue and adjusted EPS estimates, despite a slight decline in shares. Several analysts, including those from Baird, Wells Fargo, Barclays, and Telsey Advisory Group, subsequently raised their price targets for the company, maintaining Outperform or Overweight ratings based on the solid performance and future guidance.

feed.title: Mama's Creations, Inc. Experiences Revision in Stock Evaluation Amid Market Dynamics
feed.summary: Mama's Creations, Inc., a microcap FMCG company, has seen its stock valuation revised following significant market dynamics. The stock closed at $15.76, demonstrating strong returns of 16.83% year-to-date and 170.79% over the past year, significantly outperforming the S&P 500. Technical indicators show mixed signals, with bullish trends in MACD and moving averages, but bearish RSI.

feed.title: Bridgford: Fiscal Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Bridgford Foods Corp. reported a loss of $843,000, or 9 cents per share, in its fiscal first quarter, with revenue reaching $55.3 million. The company's shares have declined almost 7% since the start of the year and 30% over the last 12 months. This report was generated using data from Zacks Investment Research.

feed.title: Tigress Financial raises McDonald’s stock price target on growth strategy
feed.summary: Tigress Financial Partners has increased its price target for McDonald's (NYSE: MCD) to $385, maintaining a Buy rating, citing the company's multi-year growth strategy, which includes leveraging its global brand, digital ecosystem, AI-driven efficiencies, and franchise model. This new target is in line with the analyst consensus high, despite InvestingPro data suggesting the stock might be overvalued at its current price. McDonald's consistent dividend increases for 50 years and strong financial health further support the positive outlook.

feed.title: Barclays Raises Price Target for Fastenal (FAST) to $44 | FAST S
feed.summary: Barclays has maintained an "Equal-Weight" rating for Fastenal (FAST) while increasing its price target from $43.00 to $44.00, representing a 2.33% raise. This adjustment was made by analyst Guy Hardwick. Despite this, the average target price from 12 analysts is $44.85, implying a 3.00% downside from the current price, and GuruFocus estimates a 5.13% downside based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Williams-Sonoma portfolio brand GreenRow unveils first retail store
feed.summary: Williams-Sonoma Inc.'s brand, GreenRow, has opened its first physical retail store in New York City's SoHo neighborhood. The store, located at 47 Howard St., is designed to resemble a lived-in home and showcases GreenRow's mission-driven approach to sustainably crafted home furnishings. GreenRow, launched in 2023, emphasizes innovative and sustainable manufacturing practices, with a portion of opening weekend sales benefiting the New York Botanical Garden.

feed.title: Hooker Furnishings Declares Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Hooker Furnishings Corporation (Nasdaq-GS: HOFT) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.115 per share. The dividend is payable on March 31, 2026, to shareholders of record on March 16, 2026. The company is a designer, marketer, and importer of various furniture types for residential, hospitality, and contract markets, also manufacturing premium custom leather and fabric-upholstered, and outdoor furniture domestically.

feed.title: Dr Pepper’s New Moves: What You Need To Know Before Your Next Sip
feed.summary: Dr Pepper is aggressively expanding its market presence with new flavors, collaborations, and zero-sugar options, targeting Gen Z and Millennials across various US retail channels and social media. The article details Dr Pepper's repositioning as a distinctive alternative to traditional colas, highlighting its flavor identity, market availability, and consumer perception. It also includes a reality check on health aspects and how Dr Pepper compares to major competitors.

feed.title: Conagra plans $220 million expansion at its Fayetteville plant
feed.summary: Conagra Brands announced a $220 million investment to expand its chicken production capacity at its Fayetteville, Arkansas plant. This expansion is expected to create over 100 new jobs within the next five years, reinforcing Conagra's commitment to its frozen food business and the region's manufacturing sector. The Fayetteville plant currently produces 15 million cases of ready-to-eat meals annually for various brands like Hungry-Man and Healthy Choice.

feed.title: BJ's Restaurants Director Buys $42,120 in Stock
feed.summary: BJ's Restaurants Director C Bradford Richmond purchased 1,200 shares of the company's stock for a total of $42,120. This transaction, occurring on March 3rd, 2026, increased his ownership stake by 7.74% to 16,700 shares. The insider purchase signals the director's confidence in the company's future prospects and could be a positive indicator for its performance.

feed.title: Smithfield Foods and Harris Teeter Donate 40,000 Pounds of Protein to Food Bank of Central & Eastern North Carolina
feed.summary: Smithfield Foods and Harris Teeter partnered to donate nearly 40,000 pounds of protein to the Food Bank of Central & Eastern North Carolina. This donation, equivalent to over 160,000 servings, supports the Food Bank's efforts to combat food insecurity in its 34-county service area. This initiative is part of Smithfield's "Helping Hungry Homes" program, which has provided over 130 million servings of protein since 2008.

feed.title: Smithfield Foods Donates 40,000 Pounds of Protein to Utah Food Bank
feed.summary: Smithfield Foods and Smith's Food & Drug have partnered to donate over 40,000 pounds of protein to the Utah Food Bank as part of Smithfield's "Helping Hungry Homes" initiative. This donation, equivalent to more than 160,000 servings, aims to combat hunger in Utah where one in eight residents struggle with food insecurity. Representatives from both companies emphasized the importance of such partnerships in alleviating hunger and supporting local communities.

feed.title: Birkenstock Holding PLC (BIRK) Trading Down 3.83% on Mar 6
feed.summary: Shares of Birkenstock Holding PLC (BIRK) dropped 3.83% on March 6, trading down to $38.65, significantly below its 52-week high. Analysts, however, project an average target price of $58.24 for BIRK, suggesting a potential upside of over 50% from its current price and an "Outperform" consensus recommendation. This report was generated using automated technology and GuruFocus financial data.

feed.title: Inspired Entertainment (INSE) Launches Soccer 3.6 Across Greece
feed.summary: Inspired Entertainment (INSE) has expanded its presence in the Greek gaming market by launching its new Virtual Sports game, Soccer 3.6, in collaboration with Allwyn Hellas. The game is now available in approximately 3,000 retail locations across Greece. Despite this strategic expansion, the company faces financial challenges, including high leverage, declining margins, and a low Altman Z-Score indicating potential financial distress, necessitating a cautious approach for investors.

feed.title: Is Penske Automotive Group (PAG) Offering Value After Recent Share Price Stability
feed.summary: Penske Automotive Group (PAG) shares are currently trading at US$157.66 and appear undervalued based on two valuation approaches. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests a 25.7% discount to its intrinsic value of $212.11 per share, while its P/E ratio of 11.1x is below Simply Wall St's "Fair Ratio" of 14.9x, indicating undervaluation. The article also presents bull and bear case narratives for investor consideration.

feed.title: Texas Roadhouse adds White Castle CEO to the board
feed.summary: Texas Roadhouse has appointed Lisa Ingram, CEO of White Castle, to its board of directors. Ingram, a fourth-generation leader of the 105-year-old White Castle brand, has been CEO since 2015. Texas Roadhouse CEO Jerry Morgan highlighted Ingram's extensive experience and "people-first" leadership as valuable assets to the company.

feed.title: VO, CRH, TDG, CMI: ETF Inflow Alert
feed.summary: The Vanguard Mid-Cap ETF (VO) experienced a significant inflow of approximately $961.7 million, marking a 1.0% increase in outstanding units week-over-week. This inflow indicates growing investor demand for the ETF. The article also notes the performance of some of VO's underlying components, with CRH plc and Cummins, Inc. down, while TransDigm Group Inc is up.

feed.title: Advance Auto to Present Next Week
feed.summary: Advance Auto Parts, Inc. (NYSE: AAP) announced that its CEO, Shane O'Kelly, and CFO, Ryan Grimsland, will present at the 2026 UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference on Wednesday, March 11, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. ET. The presentation will be webcast live on the company's Investor Relations website. As of January 3, 2026, Advance Auto operated 4,305 stores primarily in the United States, along with additional locations in Canada, Puerto Rico, and the U.S. Virgin Islands, and also served 809 independently owned Carquest branded stores.

feed.title: We Expect JD To Register Non-GAAP Net Income Growth of 2% in 2026, After Heavy Investments in 2025
feed.summary: Morningstar expects JD to achieve non-GAAP net income growth of 2% in 2026, following substantial investments made in 2025. These investments include a CNY 10 billion subsidy program to attract price-sensitive customers and the launch of a food delivery business to expand its user base and diversify revenue. Despite anticipated weak sales growth in 2026 due to pulled-forward demand from the subsidy program, JD continues to focus on its asset-heavy first-party model complemented by a third-party model.

feed.title: Investors heavily search Walmart Inc. (WMT): Here is what you need to know
feed.summary: This article indicates that Walmart Inc. (WMT) is currently a popular stock among investors, suggesting high interest in the company. It implies that investors are actively seeking information about WMT, making it a frequently searched ticker symbol. The article sets the expectation of providing essential information for those interested in the stock.

feed.title: Williams-Sonoma portfolio brand GreenRow unveils first retail store
feed.summary: GreenRow, a Williams-Sonoma Inc. brand, has opened its first brick-and-mortar retail store in New York City's SoHo neighborhood. The store is designed to resemble a lived-in home, showcasing responsibly crafted home furnishings made from sustainable materials. This marks a significant step for the brand, bringing its mission-driven approach to a physical retail experience, with a portion of opening weekend sales benefiting the New York Botanical Garden.

feed.title: A Look At Pool Corporation (POOL) Valuation As Capital Returns And Dividend Commitment Draw Interest
feed.summary: Pool Corporation's valuation is under scrutiny following a significant share price decline, despite the company reaffirming its dividend commitment with a $1.25 per share payment. While the narrative fair value suggests POOL is 17.4% undervalued at $263.70 with a consensus price target of $333.27, its P/E ratio of 19.8x is above the industry average and its own fair ratio, indicating potential valuation risk. Investors are encouraged to consider both the company's dividend reliability and the valuation challenges before making investment decisions.

feed.title: Kroger names 84.51° chief to oversee AI efforts
feed.summary: Kroger has appointed Milen Mahadevan, president of its 84.51° analytics unit, as its new chief data and AI officer, a newly created role. Mahadevan will lead the company's AI initiatives, focusing on leveraging data assets, developing "agentic capabilities," and integrating AI into internal processes and customer engagement while continuing his role at 84.51°. This move highlights Kroger's serious commitment to AI, with significant investments planned for 2026 and beyond to maximize this opportunity.

feed.title: Cencora, Inc. Experiences Revision in Stock Evaluation Amid Strong Market Performance
feed.summary: Cencora, Inc. has recently undergone a revised stock evaluation. Despite a slight decline in its current stock price, the company has shown strong market performance over the past year with a 42.84% return, significantly outperforming the S&P 500. Technical indicators show mixed signals, with weekly and monthly bullish trends but a mildly bearish KST on a weekly basis, while Dow Theory supports a mildly bullish stance.

feed.title: Costco Earnings: Margin Expansion and Membership Growth Shine
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale reported strong fiscal-second-quarter results, with net sales growing 9.1% and comparable sales up 6.7%. The company saw significant membership fee income growth of 14% and expansion in its gross margins, reinforcing its wide economic moat. While Morningstar plans to raise its fair value estimate for Costco, it views the stock as overvalued given current market pricing implies unrealistic EPS growth expectations.

feed.title: Conagra Brands to Expand Manufacturing Operations in Fayetteville, Arkansas
feed.summary: Conagra Brands is investing approximately $220 million to expand its manufacturing facility in Fayetteville, Arkansas, a project expected to create over 100 new jobs within the next five years. This expansion will significantly increase the company's chicken production capacity, reinforcing its commitment to the local community and supporting innovation in its protein portfolio. The move was praised by state and local officials, highlighting Fayetteville's role as a hub for food innovation and manufacturing.

feed.title: Henry Schein Opens Dallas Discovery Center
feed.summary: Henry Schein has opened its new Dallas Discovery Center, an 8,300-square-foot training facility for healthcare education, technology, and innovation. The center aims to integrate dental and medical training, fostering a more patient-centric approach to healthcare delivery. It features state-of-the-art facilities for demonstrating equipment, holding immersive learning environments, and strengthening team expertise.

feed.title: T-Mobile Unveils "Spring Surge" Promos: iPhone 17e and iPad Air Bundles Signal Shift Toward Ecosystem ARPU
feed.summary: T-Mobile has launched its "Spring Surge" promotions, bundling the new iPhone 17e and M4 iPad Air with its premium "Experience Beyond" service plans. This strategy aims to shift revenue growth from subscriber counts to increased "device-attach" rates and Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) by making Apple's ecosystem more accessible through higher-tier plans that no longer include taxes and fees. T-Mobile, Apple, Verizon, and AT&T are positioned differently in this new competitive landscape, where T-Mobile's move is seen as a calculated risk to secure long-term gains in the evolving 5G market.

feed.title: Yum! Brands Inc.: Is This Fast-Food Giant Still Worth Your Money?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Yum! Brands Inc., the parent company of KFC, Taco Bell, and Pizza Hut, to assess its investment potential. It highlights Yum!'s asset-light franchise model, global presence, and dividend appeal, while also addressing concerns about shifting consumer habits, competitive pressures, and limited growth potential for those seeking high-growth stocks. The piece concludes that Yum! Brands is a stable, income-oriented play for investors seeking defensive consumer exposure and dividends.

feed.title: CFOs On the Move: Week ending March 6
feed.summary: This article details several significant CFO appointments and movements across various companies for the week ending March 6. Key changes include Delta Air Lines' CFO Dan Janki moving to COO, L3Harris Technologies appointing Ken Sharp as its new finance chief, and McKesson hiring Kenny Cheung from Sysco. The piece also covers new CFOs for Thomson Reuters, Asana, Wyndham Hotels & Resorts, Eaton, and Red Lobster, highlighting the dynamic nature of finance leadership roles in major organizations.

feed.title: Autohome (ATHM) Q4 Earnings Meet Estimates at $0.09 EPS Despite Revenue Plunge to $209M
feed.summary: Autohome (ATHM) reported Q4 2025 adjusted diluted EPS of $0.09, meeting analyst expectations, despite a sharp year-over-year revenue decline to $209.1 million. The company is accelerating its transformation into a transaction platform with the launch of Autohome Mall and expansion into new electric vehicle retail. Management emphasized a strong commitment to shareholder returns, announcing a new $200 million share repurchase program and sustained dividends.

feed.title: Celebrate Academy Sports + Outdoors Run Month with Exclusive Deals, Promotions and Gifts for Runners of All Experience Levels
feed.summary: Academy Sports + Outdoors has announced its "Run Month" celebration, running from March 1st to March 31st, offering deals, online promotions, and in-store fit clinics for runners of all levels. The event features an expanded selection of running gear from top brands and includes a partnership with Girls on the Run to donate $20,000 worth of running shoes to 80 youth athletes. Customers can also benefit from personalized shoe fittings, free gifts with purchase, competitive price matching, and rewards for credit cardholders and myAcademy Rewards members.

feed.title: Academy Sports (ASO) Boosts Dividend by 15.4%
feed.summary: Academy Sports and Outdoors (ASO) has announced a 15.4% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.15 per share, resulting in a forward yield of 0.99%. The company also plans significant expansion, with 20-25 new stores expected by 2026. Despite recent insider selling, ASO demonstrates strong financial health and a strategic growth outlook.

feed.title: Wave bye to 170-year-old Dick's Sporting Good rival as chain closes 36 stores
feed.summary: The Orvis Company, a 170-year-old sporting goods retailer and competitor to Dick's Sporting Goods, has announced the closure of 36 stores due to rising costs and tariffs. While Orvis focuses on core strengths, other retailers like Sportman’s Warehouse, Grocery Outlet, GameStop, Francesca's, Walgreens, and Kroger are also reducing their brick-and-mortar footprint across the US. This trend highlights a larger exodus of physical stores facing similar financial pressures.

feed.title: High Tide Opens 220th Canna Cabana in Sarnia, Ontario
feed.summary: High Tide Inc. has announced the opening of its 220th Canna Cabana retail cannabis store in Sarnia, Ontario, marking its first entry into the Sarnia market and 96th location in the province. The new store, situated on London Road, is strategically placed in a well-established commercial area. Raj Grover, High Tide's CEO, highlighted Sarnia's strong demographic profile for cannabis retail, expecting their discount club model to appeal to local consumers and noting continued opportunities for organic expansion across Canada.

feed.title: Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) Margin Compression Tests Bullish Long Term Profit Narratives
feed.summary: Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) reported solid Q3 FY 2026 results with strong revenue and EPS, but experienced a slight net margin compression from 11.2% to 10.1%. While bullish narratives point to significant five-year earnings growth, concerns arise regarding cost pressures and analysts' projections for further margin decline. The stock trades at a lower P/E ratio compared to peers, with a wide range between its DCF fair value and current price, reflecting diverse analyst opinions on its future profitability.

feed.title: Parade and Steller Announce Finalists for America's Favorite Small Town
feed.summary: Parade and Steller have announced the 40 finalists in their search for America's Favorite Small Town, a competition celebrating communities with populations under 250,000. These towns, chosen for their compelling stories and public support, will receive national exposure on Parade and Steller platforms. Regional winners will be announced on June 1, 2026, and a national winner on June 12, 2026, with the grand prize including a Parade x Steller activation at the winning town's July 4th celebration.

feed.title: Traeger (NYSE:COOK) Price Target Lowered to $0.75 at Piper Sandler
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has lowered its price target for Traeger (NYSE:COOK) from $1.50 to $0.75, maintaining a "neutral" rating. This adjustment comes after Traeger's Q4 earnings reported mixed results, with revenue beating estimates but EPS missing, and the company significantly reduced its Q1 and full-year 2026 revenue guidance. The new price target implies only a modest upside from the current share price, while the broader analyst consensus suggests a "Reduce" rating with a higher average target.

feed.title: 5 Top Leisure and Recreation Stocks to Consider Buying as Demand Surges in 2026
feed.summary: The leisure and recreation sector is experiencing strong growth due to factors like streamlined operations, collaborations, digital advancements, and increased consumer spending. This article highlights five top leisure and recreation stocks, all with Zacks Ranks of #1 or #2, that present promising investment opportunities. These companies include Expedia Group Inc. (EXPE), Viking Holdings Ltd. (VIK), Johnson Outdoors Inc. (JOUT), Callaway Golf Co. (CALY), and MasterCraft Boat Holdings Inc. (MCFT), with each demonstrating strong financial performance and strategic growth initiatives.

feed.title: Genesco Q4 FY26 Results Show Strong Nine Percent Comp Growth
feed.summary: Genesco reported a 7% increase in fourth-quarter net sales to $800 million for Fiscal 2026, driven by a 9% rise in comparable sales. The Journeys Group led this performance, while full-year net sales reached $2.4 billion, a 5% year-over-year increase. For Fiscal 2027, the company anticipates positive comparable sales growth of 1% to 2% and adjusted diluted earnings per share between $1.90 and $2.30.

feed.title: Burlington Stores (NYSE:BURL) Given New $400.00 Price Target at Wells Fargo & Company
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has increased its price target for Burlington Stores (NYSE:BURL) to $400.00 from $350.00, maintaining an "overweight" rating. This upgrade follows Burlington's Q4 earnings beat and raised FY2026 guidance, despite Q1 guidance falling below consensus and increased put option activity signaling potential near-term volatility. The average price target from analysts for BURL is now $352.13, with a "Moderate Buy" consensus.

feed.title: Walmart's digital price tags are coming to all US stores. What to know
feed.summary: Walmart is expanding its digital shelf labels to all U.S. stores within the next year, replacing traditional paper tags. These digital tags allow for quick price updates and help employees streamline tasks such as restocking and fulfilling online orders by using LED lights on the labels to locate items. Walmart first piloted these labels in 2024 and has since expanded them to thousands of locations.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty Q4 Earnings on Deck: Should Investors Expect a Beat?
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty (ULTA) is expected to report increased Q4 fiscal 2025 revenues on March 12, driven by its "Ulta Beauty Unleashed" strategy, loyalty base, and merchandising innovations. Despite top-line growth, the company anticipates a decline in earnings per share due to higher SG&A expenses, technology investments, and a cautious macroeconomic environment. However, with a Zacks Rank #3 and a positive Earnings ESP of +11.98%, Ulta Beauty is predicted to beat earnings estimates.

feed.title: Is Kimberly-Clark Stock Underperforming the Dow?
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark stock, despite outperforming the Dow Jones Industrials Average in the last three months and year-to-date, has lagged over the past 52 weeks, dipping 27.4% compared to Dow's 11.5% increase. The company reported strong Q4 2025 results with increased EPS and operating profit, along with a positive 2026 outlook. Analysts currently have a "Moderate Buy" rating for KMB, with a mean price target indicating a potential 13.5% premium.

feed.title: UniFirst Corporation Stock Jumps After Report Says Cintas Is Nearing a Deal Above $275 a Share
feed.summary: UniFirst Corporation's stock jumped after a Bloomberg Law report indicated Cintas is nearing a deal to acquire the workwear rival for over $275 per share, an offer previously made in December. Despite prior negotiation hurdles, the potential acquisition would value UniFirst at approximately $5.2 billion and aimed at boosting operational efficiency for both companies. The deal still faces challenges including shareholder approval, UniFirst's dual-class share structure, and potential antitrust concerns, though Cintas has proposed a $350 million reverse termination fee.

feed.title: Avery Dennison Launches AD IdentiFresh™ to Unlock Efficiency, Freshness and Waste Reduction in Food Retail
feed.summary: Avery Dennison has launched its AD IdentiFresh™ inlay series, an RFID technology designed to improve efficiency, freshness, and reduce waste in fresh food categories like bakery, meat, deli, and produce. This innovation, developed in collaboration with partners like Impinj, overcomes operational challenges in cold, high-moisture environments. The new series aims to digitize and automate fresh food management from production to point of sale, addressing significant food waste identified in recent research.

feed.title: Coca-Cola (KO) Reports 2025 Net Revenue of $47.9B, Full-Year EPS of $3.04
feed.summary: The Coca-Cola Company reported a 2% increase in net revenues to $47.9 billion for full-year 2025, with organic revenues up 5%. Despite a Q4 operating income decline due to an impairment charge, full-year operating income rose 38% and EPS hit $3.04. The company expects 4% to 5% organic revenue growth and 7% to 8% comparable EPS growth in 2026, alongside $12.2 billion in free cash flow and continued dividend increases.

feed.title: MercadoLibre (MELI) Reports 45% Q4 Revenue Growth, Full-Year Operating Income Up 22%
feed.summary: MercadoLibre (MELI) reported a 45% year-over-year increase in Q4 net revenues, although its EPS of $11.03 missed analyst expectations. The company's full-year operating income grew by 22%, driven by investments in commerce and fintech ecosystems, including shipping subsidies and credit card expansion in Latin America. AI-powered algorithms significantly boosted advertising growth and customer service efficiency, while its fintech arm, Mercado Pago, expanded its credit portfolio and issued 3 million new credit cards in Q4.

feed.title: Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) Given New $44.00 Price Target at Barclays
feed.summary: Barclays has raised its price target for Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) to $44.00 from $43.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating, despite suggesting a potential 6% downside from its previous close. The analyst consensus for Fastenal remains a "Hold" with an average price target of $48.83. The company recently reported Q4 earnings per share of $0.26, meeting expectations, and revenue of $2.03 billion, which was up 11.1% year-over-year but slightly below consensus estimates.

feed.title: LKQ Has Outperform Rating on LKQ Corporation (LKQ)
feed.summary: Barrington maintained an "Outperform" rating on LKQ Corporation (LKQ) and increased its target price range to $45-$50 after the company's Q4 2025 earnings report. Despite a soft demand environment in North America and Europe, which led to a decline in Q4 profits and missed street expectations, analysts remain optimistic. The company, a specialty retailer of automotive parts, faced rising costs and price increases that impacted sales volume and gross margins.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark names Francesco Tinto as chief information & GBS officer
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark Corp. has appointed Francesco Tinto as chief information & global business services (GBS) officer, effective March 9, 2026. Tinto will report to Russ Torres, president and chief operating officer, and will lead the company's Information Technology and GBS organizations. He brings over 30 years of technology leadership experience from companies including Advantage Solutions, Walgreens Boots Alliance, and Kraft Heinz, and will contribute to Kimberly-Clark's Powering Care strategy.

feed.title: Birmingham’s Coca-Cola United board chairman to retire after 47 years
feed.summary: Conrad W. Rafield III, the longest-serving director in Coca-Cola United’s 124-year history, will retire as chairman of the board on April 28 after 47 years with the company. John H. Sherman III, former president and CEO, has been elected to succeed him, pending shareholder approval. Sherman would be the first non-Johnson family member to hold the chairman position.

feed.title: Academy Sports + Outdoors Increases Quarterly Cash Dividend
feed.summary: Academy Sports and Outdoors, Inc. announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.15 per share for the fiscal quarter ended January 31, 2026. This represents a 15 percent increase from the previous dividend, marking the fourth consecutive year of dividend growth. The dividend is payable on April 10, 2026, to stockholders of record as of March 20, 2026.

feed.title: GreenRow turns SoHo shop into a lived-in home for sustainable design fans
feed.summary: Williams-Sonoma's GreenRow has opened its first brick-and-mortar store in SoHo, New York City, designed to resemble a lived-in home to showcase its sustainably crafted home furnishings. The store emphasizes quality, sustainability, and timeless design through products made with responsibly sourced materials. GreenRow aims to provide an immersive retail experience for customers who care about how products are made and how they integrate into their homes.

feed.title: Jefferies cuts Flutter price target by 45% but keeps 'buy' rating, arguing market prices in zero US growth
feed.summary: Jefferies has maintained its 'buy' rating for Flutter Entertainment PLC despite significantly cutting its price target by 45% from $380 to $210 after the gambling group's full-year results revealed margin pressures. The broker believes the market has overreacted to short-term issues, pricing in zero future US growth which contradicts available evidence. Flutter's stock was up about 1% at 8,609.64p following the updated guidance.

feed.title: Aramark Celebrates Standout Partners Powering Local Restaurant Row in 2025
feed.summary: Aramark is celebrating the standout partners who will be powering the Local Restaurant Row in 2025. This initiative highlights the company's commitment to collaborating with local businesses to enhance its service offerings.

feed.title: DA Davidson Initiates Coverage on Wingstop (WING) with a Buy Rating
feed.summary: DA Davidson has initiated coverage on Wingstop (WING) with a "Buy" rating and a $330.00 price target, reflecting a positive outlook on the company. This initiation aligns with recent "Buy" and "Overweight" ratings from other analysts, collectively indicating strong consensus on Wingstop's growth potential. Wingstop, a fast-casual restaurant focused on chicken, primarily operates as a franchisor, with the majority of its revenue derived from royalties and advertising fees from its 2,563 units.

feed.title: Inspired and Allwyn Hellas Launch Soccer 3.6 Across 3,000 Retail Venues in Greece
feed.summary: Inspired Entertainment has partnered with Allwyn Hellas to launch its new Virtual Sports product, Soccer 3.6™, across approximately 3,000 retail venues in Greece. The game introduces a Bet Builder functionality, allowing players to bet on multiple event outcomes in the same game, enhancing engagement. This collaboration reinforces Inspired's leadership in Virtual Sports and its commitment to developing tailored content for its partners.

feed.title: How iPhone 17e becomes a rugged warehouse barcode scanner
feed.summary: Socket Mobile announced that its industrial scanning solutions, including XtremeScan, DuraSled, and DuraScan Terminal, are now compatible with Apple's new iPhone 17e. This collaboration enables businesses to use the enhanced durability and performance of the iPhone 17e for 1D/2D barcode capture in demanding environments across various industries. The company's CaptureSDK facilitates easy integration for developers, allowing for quick deployment of iPhone 17e-based data capture systems.

feed.title: After Logo Fiasco, Cracker Barrel Lays Out Plans to Rebuild Its Business
feed.summary: Cracker Barrel announced plans to revamp its business after reporting a 94 percent profit drop following a contested logo change. CEO Julie Felss Masino stated the company is taking steps to update menus and modernize store aesthetics. These efforts, along with an updated full-year outlook, caused shares to rise almost 8 percent in after-hours trading.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Reassess American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) After Its 92% One Year Surge?
feed.summary: American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) has seen a 92% surge over the past year, prompting an assessment of its current valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 17.0% compared to its current price of US$22.24, with an intrinsic value of $26.78 per share. Additionally, its P/E ratio of 18.1x is below the industry average, further suggesting it may be undervalued.

feed.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights Liquidity Services, Remitly Global, Exponent and Sezzle
feed.summary: The Zacks Analyst Blog identifies four stocks—Liquidity Services (LQDT), Remitly Global (RELY), Exponent (EXPO), and Sezzle (SEZL)—as strong investment opportunities within the services sector. These companies are highlighted due to robust growth in the U.S. non-manufacturing sector, which expanded significantly in February. The article details each company's expected earnings growth and their current Zacks Rank (Strong Buy or Buy), recommending them to investors looking to boost their portfolios.

feed.title: DA Davidson Initiates Coverage on LOCO with Neutral Rating | LOC
feed.summary: DA Davidson has initiated coverage on El Pollo Loco Holdings (LOCO) with a Neutral rating, without setting an immediate price target. This follows recent analyst activities where Truist Securities maintained a Hold rating while raising its price target, and Jefferies maintained a Buy rating with an increased price target. The average target price from five analysts for LOCO is $14.30, indicating a potential upside of 27.56% from its current price, while GuruFocus estimates a one-year GF Value of $12.18, suggesting an 8.65% upside.

feed.title: Analyst DA Davidson Initiates Neutral Coverage on CAVA | CAVA St
feed.summary: DA Davidson has initiated coverage on Cava Group (CAVA) with a neutral rating. This follows a series of recent analyst actions with mixed ratings, including upgrades to price targets by Keybanc, TD Cowen, Piper Sandler, and RBC Capital, and a downgrade by Argus Research. The average target price from 20 analysts for CAVA is $81.67, implying a 2.89% upside, while GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $96.42, suggesting a 21.48% upside.

feed.title: Couture-inspired kids occasionwear in Marchesa Mini x Gymboree
feed.summary: The Children’s Place (Nasdaq: PLCE) and Marchesa have launched "MARCHESA MINI X GYMBOREE," a collaboration offering couture-inspired occasionwear for girls. Debuted on February 19, 2026, the collection combines Marchesa’s sophisticated silhouettes with Gymboree’s child-first design, focusing on wearable luxury, comfort, and memory-making details. This initiative is part of PLCE’s strategy to elevate the Gymboree brand through special occasion collaborations, with pieces available online and in select stores.

feed.title: Twin Vee PowerCats Set to Redefine the Offshore Adventure Day Boat Category
feed.summary: Twin Vee PowerCats Co. announced its new Adventure Series, a line of power catamarans designed to revolutionize the offshore adventure day boat market. These American-built boats aim to combine the popular lifestyle-oriented adventure concept with the superior performance and stability of a power catamaran. The series will feature multiple models focusing on versatility, entertaining, and multi-activity use, offering enhanced features compared to traditional monohull boats.

feed.title: Twin Vee PowerCats Set to Redefine the Offshore Adventure Day Boat Category
feed.summary: Twin Vee PowerCats Co. announced its new Adventure Series, a lineup of power catamarans designed to redefine the offshore adventure day boat market. The company aims to combine the adventure boat concept with the performance advantages of a power catamaran, offering superior offshore ride quality, fuel efficiency, stability, and more usable deck space. The Adventure Series will include various models from 24 to 40 feet, featuring lifestyle-oriented amenities for entertaining, watersports, and family recreation, extending Twin Vee's appeal beyond traditional offshore fishing enthusiasts.

feed.title: Watch Advance Auto Parts leaders at UBS 2026 consumer and retail conference
feed.summary: Advance Auto Parts (NYSE: AAP) announced that its President and CEO, Shane O'Kelly, and EVP and CFO, Ryan Grimsland, will present at the 2026 UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference on March 11, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. ET. The presentation will be webcast live on the company's Investor Relations website, with a replay available afterward. This event provides an opportunity for investors to hear directly from company leadership regarding their strategies and performance.

feed.title: Advance Auto Parts to Present at the 2026 UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference
feed.summary: Advance Auto Parts, Inc. announced that its President and CEO, Shane O'Kelly, and CFO, Ryan Grimsland, will present at the 2026 UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference. The presentation is scheduled for March 11, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. ET and will be available via live webcast on the company's Investor Relations website, with a replay accessible afterward. Advance Auto Parts is a leading automotive aftermarket parts provider serving both professional installers and do-it-yourself customers.

feed.title: Vail Resorts's Earnings: A Preview
feed.summary: Vail Resorts (NYSE: MTN) is set to release its quarterly earnings on March 9, 2026, with analysts anticipating an EPS of $6.12. Investors will be focused on guidance for the next quarter, as past performance shows a significant share price increase when the company beats EPS estimates. Despite healthy market capitalization, revenue growth, and net margin, the company faces challenges with its stock down 8.68% over the last year, and a high debt-to-equity ratio alongside a low return on equity.

feed.title: UBS lowers PVH stock price target on margin concerns, keeps buy
feed.summary: UBS has lowered its price target for PVH Corp (NYSE:PVH) from $148 to $120, while maintaining a Buy rating on the stock, citing concerns about gross margins. Despite the reduced target, UBS expects PVH to report solid fourth-quarter earnings and provide strong fiscal 2026 guidance, which they believe will boost the stock's valuation. The firm also noted PVH's recent partnership with OpenAI to integrate AI into its operations, aiming to enhance product design, demand planning, and customer engagement.

feed.title: Proud to be American: American Airlines partners with America250 to celebrate nation’s 250th anniversary
feed.summary: American Airlines has announced a partnership with America250, the national organization leading the commemoration of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. This collaboration will see American Airlines, which is also celebrating its centennial in 2026, play a significant role in the nationwide celebration of the United States' 250th anniversary, including painting two aircraft in America250 livery and serving as the Official Airline of America Innovates. The partnership underscores American's deep ties to U.S. history and its commitment to connecting communities.

feed.title: American Rebel brings light beer and live rock to NHRA season opener
feed.summary: American Rebel Holdings (NASDAQ:AREB) and American Rebel Light Beer are the Official Beer Sponsor of the 57th AMALIE Motor Oil NHRA Gatornationals (March 5–8, 2026) in Gainesville, marking the start of NHRA's 75th anniversary season. The event will feature an on-site American Rebel Beer Garden, live music performances by CEO Andy Ross, and continued partnership with Tony Stewart Racing, showcasing American Rebel Light Beer with its 110 calories, 4g carbs, and 4.2% ABV. Despite the significant sponsorship, AREB stock experienced an 8.17% decline following the news.

feed.title: Acadia Realty Trust (AKR) Valuation Check After Recent Share Price Gains
feed.summary: Acadia Realty Trust (AKR) has seen its stock price rise by around 7% in the past month and 9% in the last three months, capturing investor attention. Despite a 1% decline over the past year, the company's 3-year and 5-year returns show significant momentum, driven by a reassessment of its retail-focused real estate portfolio's income potential. Simply Wall St estimates AKR to be 6.5% undervalued with a fair value of $22.86, but notes the P/S ratio indicates a tighter story, suggesting investors might be paying for quality rather than getting a bargain.

feed.title: First Watch Restaurant Group (NASDAQ:FWRG) Coverage Initiated at DA Davidson
feed.summary: DA Davidson has initiated coverage on First Watch Restaurant Group (FWRG) with a "buy" rating, aligning with a "Moderate Buy" consensus from analysts and an average price target of $20.08, despite recent adjustments. The company recently reported strong quarterly results, beating EPS expectations ($0.24 vs. $0.08) and achieving a 20.2% year-over-year revenue increase to $316.35 million, which led to an 8.4% intraday stock jump. Institutional ownership is high at approximately 96%, with major investors like Vanguard increasing their stakes, while insiders hold about 6%.

feed.title: DA Davidson Initiates Coverage on First Watch Restaurant Gr (FWRG) with a Buy Rating | FWRG Stock News
feed.summary: DA Davidson has initiated coverage on First Watch Restaurant Group (FWRG) with a Buy rating. This new rating comes amid several other analyst updates, with many having recently lowered price targets while maintaining their overall ratings. The average analyst price target suggests a 50.29% upside from the current price, classifying FWRG with an "Outperform" status, while GuruFocus estimates a 110.56% upside based on its fair value calculations.

feed.title: Zuckerman Signs T.J. Maxx, Ulta Beauty at Mixed-Use Development in Southwest Florida
feed.summary: The Zuckerman Group has secured T.J. Maxx and Ulta Beauty as new tenants for Midtown at Bonita, a 68-acre mixed-use development in Bonita Springs, Florida. These additions bring the leased retail space to 100,000 square feet, complementing a variety of other committed retailers, restaurants, and services. The retail component's construction began in November, with initial tenant move-ins expected this spring, and the full build-out will also include a boutique hotel, luxury apartments, and preserved open space.

feed.title: Is Flowers Foods (FLO) Quietly Recasting Its Brand Strategy With Simple Mills Leadership And Dividends?
feed.summary: Flowers Foods (FLO) is undergoing leadership changes with two long-time board members retiring and Michelle Lorge appointed president of its Simple Mills subsidiary. The company also approved a higher quarterly dividend. These changes come as Flowers Foods aims to shift towards premium, health-oriented brands to offset pressure on its legacy bread business and recent earnings volatility, with a focus on innovation and integration under scrutiny.

feed.title: Why Reborn Coffee’s new co-CEO is focused on a $20M logistics opportunity
feed.summary: Reborn Coffee (REBN) has appointed Jung Jae Lim as Co-Chief Executive Officer to bolster its logistics and supply chain operations. Lim brings over 20 years of experience and has already secured partnerships in the USA and Mexico with an estimated $20 million in annual logistics revenue potential. This strategic move aims to strengthen the company's operational foundation and accelerate enterprise partnerships, building on previous efforts to expand its infrastructure and market presence.

feed.title: South Dakota Investment Council Lowers Stake in Tapestry, Inc. $TPR
feed.summary: The South Dakota Investment Council reduced its stake in Tapestry, Inc. (NYSE:TPR) by 71.4% during the third quarter, while several other major institutional investors increased their holdings. Tapestry recently exceeded quarterly earnings expectations, issued strong FY2026 guidance, and authorized a $1.0 billion share buyback alongside a quarterly dividend. Insider selling has occurred, but institutional ownership remains high at 90.77%.

feed.title: Kroger forecasts modest 2026 growth as leadership transition continues
feed.summary: Kroger expects identical sales excluding fuel to grow between 1% and 2% in 2026, forecasting earnings per share of $5.10 to $5.30 and free cash flow of $2.7bn to $2.9bn. The company's net earnings for fiscal year 2025 dropped significantly to $1.01bn from $2.66bn, largely due to impairment and related charges linked to its automated fulfillment network. New CEO Greg Foran aims to strengthen the company by enhancing customer value, improving their in-store and online experience, and driving cost savings.

feed.title: Grainger expands in SubTropolis to have largest known underground distribution center
feed.summary: W.W. Grainger Inc. is set to complete a significant expansion of its distribution center in the underground SubTropolis business park by early summer. This expansion will bring their total footprint to 1.6 million square feet, making it the largest known underground distribution center. The project involves increasing their space within the unique subterranean facility in Kansas City.

feed.title: EPAM Accelerates Agentic AI Adoption for Enterprises
feed.summary: EPAM is accelerating agentic AI adoption for enterprises by helping clients move from experimentation to scalable, production-grade deployments, especially through its partnership with Microsoft. The company addresses key challenges such as data readiness and organizational alignment, leveraging its foundational work in data estate fixes and repeatable engineering practices. EPAM's efforts, including a collaboration with Albert Heijn, earned it the 2025 Innovate with Azure AI Platform Partner of the Year Award.

feed.title: EPAM Accelerates Agentic AI Adoption for Enterprises
feed.summary: EPAM is accelerating agentic AI adoption for enterprises by focusing on data readiness and organizational alignment, moving beyond pilot programs to production-grade deployments. As a Microsoft Global Systems Integrator, EPAM leverages its expertise and internal enablement, including deploying Microsoft 365 Copilot, to help clients like Albert Heijn transform business processes with AI, addressing common challenges such as inadequate data infrastructure and lack of stakeholder buy-in.

feed.title: American Express to partner with Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Falcons, Atlanta United
feed.summary: American Express has formed a long-term partnership with AMB Sports and Entertainment, becoming the official payments partner for the Atlanta Falcons, Atlanta United, and Mercedes-Benz Stadium. This deal also includes early backing for Atlanta's future women's professional soccer club. Cardholders will receive benefits such as early ticket access, dedicated entry lanes, and statement credits on concessions.

feed.title: Investors Heavily Search Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN): Here is What You Need to Know
feed.summary: Amazon (AMZN) has been a highly searched stock on Zacks.com, prompting an analysis of key factors influencing its performance. The article delves into earnings estimate revisions, projected revenue growth, and valuation, noting Amazon's Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) rating. This suggests the stock is expected to perform in line with the broader market in the near term.

feed.title: Chipotle Mexican Grill (NYSE:CMG) Now Covered by DA Davidson
feed.summary: DA Davidson has initiated coverage on Chipotle Mexican Grill (NYSE:CMG) with a "Buy" rating and a $51 price target, indicating a potential 37.7% upside. This new rating contributes to a consensus of "Moderate Buy" among analysts, with an average target of $47.37. Chipotle recently surpassed earnings and revenue estimates, reporting EPS of $0.25 and revenue of $2.98 billion, and the stock currently trades around $37.

feed.title: Truepoint Inc. Purchases 14,116 Shares of Procter & Gamble Company (The) $PG
feed.summary: Truepoint Inc. increased its stake in Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) by 12.3%, purchasing an additional 14,116 shares, bringing its total holdings to 129,017 shares valued at $19.82 million. This move makes P&G Truepoint Inc.'s 26th largest holding. The article also notes recent insider selling by Chairman Jon Moeller and CEO Gary Coombe, alongside analysts' "Moderate Buy" consensus rating and the company's recent earnings beat and dividend declaration.

feed.title: Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) Given New $52.00 Price Target at Robert W. Baird
feed.summary: Robert W. Baird has increased its price target for Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) to $52.00 from $51.00, maintaining an "outperform" rating and implying a 9.94% upside. This adjustment comes amidst mixed analyst sentiment, with an average target price of $48.75 for the stock. Fastenal recently reported quarterly EPS of $0.26, meeting estimates, and revenue of $2.03 billion, slightly below estimates but up 11.1% year-over-year.

feed.title: New Kroger CEO Foran: ‘We need to grow sales faster’
feed.summary: New Kroger CEO Greg Foran has stated that the supermarket retailer needs to grow sales faster, emphasizing creating a compelling reason for customers to shop through great value, products, and experience. Foran's focus is on operationalizing strategy to enhance the business, leveraging Kroger's strengths in fresh, e-commerce, and private labels. The company reported higher adjusted earnings per share and sales for fiscal 2025, with plans to increase new store openings by 30% in 2026 and expand into two new regions.

feed.title: American Eagle Outfitters, Inc. (NYSE:AEO) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE:AEO) reported a strong fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025, with double-digit sales growth driven by robust demand for Aerie and OFFLINE, and solid progress at American Eagle. The company achieved record revenue of $1.8 billion in Q4 and $5.5 billion for the full year, with adjusted operating income up 27%. AEO remains confident in its strategy for profitable growth, investing in its core brands, and expects continued positive sales trends into 2026, despite ongoing tariff pressures and increased advertising spend in the first half.

feed.title: Victory Capital Management Inc. Decreases Stock Position in Ulta Beauty Inc. $ULTA
feed.summary: Victory Capital Management Inc. reduced its stake in Ulta Beauty Inc. by 6.0% in the third quarter, selling 7,391 shares to hold 116,632 shares valued at approximately $63.77 million. Ulta Beauty, a specialty retailer, surpassed quarterly earnings and revenue expectations, reporting robust profitability and strong year-over-year revenue growth. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for ULTA, with an average price target of $626.84.

feed.title: NextTrip Signs Agreement with J. Bradley Hilton's Hilton Advisory Group to Accelerate Premium Wellness Travel Products Across JOURNY.tv and Five Star Alliance
feed.summary: NextTrip, Inc. announced an agreement with J. Bradley Hilton's Hilton Advisory Group to enhance premium wellness travel products and content-to-commerce initiatives across its platforms, including JOURNY.tv and Five Star Alliance. Hilton Advisory Group, led by J. Bradley Hilton, will provide strategic guidance on wellness programming and curate travel experiences, leveraging Hilton's extensive hospitality background and focus on modern luxury. This collaboration aims to develop scalable travel products that align with NextTrip’s media-to-commerce vision, integrating travel discovery with booking and fulfillment.

feed.title: Victory Capital Management Inc. Has $63.45 Million Stock Holdings in Deckers Outdoor Corporation $DECK
feed.summary: Victory Capital Management Inc. decreased its stake in Deckers Outdoor Corporation by 11.9% in Q3, now holding 625,879 shares valued at approximately $63.45 million. feed.title: Sports Sponsorship Market Set to Witness Rapid Growth Through
feed.summary: Coherent Market Insights has published a report titled "Sports Sponsorship Market: Industry Trends, Share, Size, Growth, Opportunity, and Forecast, 2026-2033". The report provides an in-depth analysis of market trends, challenges, and opportunities, covering competitive dynamics, regional performance, and long-term growth potential. It serves as a comprehensive resource for stakeholders seeking market intelligence, including a SWOT analysis of leading companies like PepsiCo, Coca-Cola, Adidas, and Nike.

feed.title: Las Vegas A’s, Will Guidara, and Aramark Sports + Entertainment Reveal Vision for First-of-its-Kind Athletic Club Behind Home Plate of A’s New Ballpark
feed.summary: The Las Vegas A’s, along with restaurateur Will Guidara and Aramark Sports + Entertainment, have unveiled plans for the Athletic Club, an all-inclusive hospitality experience located behind home plate in the A’s new Las Vegas ballpark, opening in 2028. This innovative concept aims to redefine the game-day experience with premium dining, personalized service, and upscale amenities, transforming stadium visits into a comprehensive luxury event. Additionally, they introduced the Diamond Club, another exclusive social hub offering Vegas-inspired culinary experiences for premium ticket members.

feed.title: Las Vegas A’s, Will Guidara, and Aramark Sports + Entertainment Reveal Vision for First-of-its-Kind Athletic Club Behind Home Plate of A’s New Ballpark
feed.summary: The Las Vegas A’s, in collaboration with restaurateur Will Guidara and Aramark Sports + Entertainment, have unveiled plans for the "Athletic Club" and "Diamond Club" at their new ballpark opening in 2028. The Athletic Club, located behind home plate, will offer an exclusive, all-inclusive hospitality experience including a private restaurant, lounge, and personalized service, aiming to transform the traditional game-day experience. These premium spaces, inspired by Las Vegas's energy, will go on sale this month to Priority Access List members.

feed.title: Pizza powerhouse: Domino's is called undervalued by BTIG after impressive market share gains (DPZ:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: BTIG has reiterated a Buy rating on Domino's Pizza (DPZ) and considers it undervalued, largely due to impressive market share gains. The firm notes that while competitors like Papa John's and Pizza Hut face store closures and sales declines, Domino's is expected to accelerate its market share growth. Despite superior industry performance, the stock is trading near a ten-year trough valuation.

feed.title: Brinker International (NYSE:EAT) Now Covered by Analysts at DA Davidson
feed.summary: DA Davidson has initiated coverage on Brinker International (NYSE:EAT) with a "neutral" rating, though the overall analyst consensus remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average target price of $188.56. The company recently exceeded earnings expectations, reporting an EPS of $2.87 against an expected $2.53, with revenue growth of 6.9% year-over-year. Despite positive earnings, there has been notable insider selling, with executives offloading 97,678 shares worth approximately $15.7 million in the last three months.

feed.title: Brinker International (EAT) Sees Neutral Coverage Initiation by DA Davidson | EAT Stock News
feed.summary: DA Davidson has initiated coverage on Brinker International (EAT) with a 'Neutral' rating, without an associated price target. Despite these adjustments, GuruFocus's estimated GF Value suggests a potential downside for Brinker International, while Wall Street analysts, on average, forecast an "Outperform" status with a significant upside.

feed.title: A’s, Aramark, Will Guidara veer toward simplicity with first Las Vegas ballpark premium spaces
feed.summary: The A’s are preparing to sell access to their first premium spaces, The Athletic Club and Diamond Club, in their new $1.75 billion Las Vegas ballpark. Collaborating with Aramark Sports + Entertainment and hospitality expert Will Guidara, the clubs are designed for simplicity and comfort rather than Vegas's usual extravagance. The Athletic Club will be the most exclusive, offering multi-course dining and upscale services, while the Diamond Club provides a more casual, self-service food and beverage experience.

feed.title: Victory Capital Management Inc. Grows Position in The Chefs' Warehouse, Inc. $CHEF
feed.summary: Victory Capital Management Inc. increased its stake in The Chefs' Warehouse, Inc. ($CHEF) by 2.3% in Q3, holding over 1.38 million shares valued at approximately $80.7 million. Despite insider selling totaling almost $9.06 million in the last quarter, including CEO Christopher Pappas's sale of 95,908 shares, institutional investors now own about 91.55% of the company's stock. The company's Q4 results surpassed expectations with EPS of $0.68 and revenue of $1.14 billion, and analysts generally rate the stock a "Moderate Buy" with an average target price of $77.67.

feed.title: TD Asset Management Inc Invests $1.16 Million in Boot Barn Holdings, Inc. $BOOT
feed.summary: TD Asset Management Inc. has initiated a significant position in Boot Barn Holdings (NYSE:BOOT), purchasing 7,002 shares valued at approximately $1.16 million in the third quarter. This move is part of broader institutional interest, with other firms like Principal Financial Group and Fisher Asset Management also increasing their stakes. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Boot Barn, with an average price target of $217.00, following strong earnings results that met expectations and an increase in revenue.

feed.title: Victory Capital Management Inc. Raises Stake in Dole PLC $DOLE
feed.summary: Victory Capital Management Inc. significantly increased its stake in Dole PLC ($DOLE) by 30.6% in the third quarter, purchasing over 1.15 million shares to now hold 4.93 million shares valued at approximately $66.38 million. Dole has also announced a quarterly dividend of $0.085 per share and a new $100 million share buyback program. The company recently reported Q4 EPS of $0.14, beating consensus estimates, and revenue of $2.37 billion, marking a 9.2% year-over-year increase.

feed.title: Grocery Outlet Holding Corp. (NASDAQ:GO) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Grocery Outlet Holding Corp. (NASDAQ:GO) reported "unacceptable" Q4 2025 results and a challenging outlook for 2026, leading to a significant reassessment of its strategy. The company plans to close 36 underperforming stores, primarily in the East, to improve profitability and refocus resources. Management is concentrating on restoring its "opportunistic product" mix, leveraging promotions as a temporary bridge, and enhancing new store underwriting standards to drive sustainable growth and improved returns on invested capital.

feed.title: Cracker Barrel Old Country Store, Inc. (NASDAQ:CBRL) Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Cracker Barrel reported Q2 2026 total sales of $874.8 million and adjusted EBITDA of $38.2 million, acknowledging continued pressure from traffic declines but noting encouraging improvements in guest metrics like Google star ratings, food taste, service, and value scores. The company is implementing a multi-pronged strategy focusing on operational improvements, menu innovation, value propositions (including a loyalty program with over 11 million members), and cost savings through corporate restructuring and reduced advertising spend. Despite an anticipated challenging Q4 due to a strong prior-year comparison, Cracker Barrel projects full-year adjusted EBITDA of $85 million to $100 million.

feed.title: Yum China CEO’s First Share Sale Reframes Insider Ownership And Valuation

This sale provides a new dynamic for insider ownership and valuation, prompting investors to consider it alongside the company's strong revenue growth, store expansion, capital returns, and existing risks like an unstable dividend track record. The article suggests watching for future insider transactions, disclosures on capital allocation, and share price behavior to assess its impact on the long-term investment thesis.

feed.title: Yum China CEO’s First Major Sale And What It Means For Valuation

The company's stock currently trades below analyst targets and its estimated fair value, with positive recent momentum, yet it exhibits an unstable dividend record and recent significant insider selling.

feed.title: Texas Roadhouse, Inc. Appoints Lisa Ingram to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Texas Roadhouse, Inc. (Nasdaq: TXRH) has appointed Lisa Ingram, CEO and Chair of the Board for White Castle System, Inc., to its Board of Directors. Ingram leads White Castle, which operates 340 restaurants and six manufacturing plants that supply both its restaurants and consumer product goods to grocery stores nationwide. This appointment brings significant food industry leadership experience to the Texas Roadhouse board.

feed.title: Rovida Advisors Inc. Has $3.15 Million Stake in Spectrum Brands Holdings Inc. $SPB

(NYSE:SPB) by 1,140.4% in the third quarter, now holding 60,000 shares valued at approximately $3.15 million. feed.title: 1 Russell 2000 Stock with Impressive Fundamentals and 2 We Question

Conversely, it advises caution on The Marzetti Company (MZTI) and Plug Power (PLUG), citing unexciting sales, low margins, and cash burn as concerns, respectively. feed.title: FY2026 EPS Estimates for Gildan Activewear Cut by Scotiabank

feed.title: Burlington Stores, Inc. (NYSE:BURL) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript

feed.title: Target not ‘an everything store,’ CEO says

This new approach involves strengthening its unique identity in groceries by focusing on emerging brands, wellness, and own brands, with a goal to double unique food and beverage items within three years.

feed.title: Target and Walmart among stores facing checkout change as shoppers ditch carts & walk out - item limits could be next

These regulations could include requirements for staffing self-checkout lanes, limiting the number of items shoppers can scan themselves, and banning self-service for theft-deterrent products. The proposed changes arise from concerns about customers abandoning full carts due to frustration and increased retail loss.

feed.title: What Can Trigger Costco Wholesale Stock’s Slide?

This investment aims to meet the demand from sectors such as food, beverage, pet food, and healthcare, and to help companies comply with regulations requiring all packaging to be recyclable by 2030.

This immersive experience aimed to deepen emotional connections to travel and highlight Travelzoo's mission to build a community of passionate explorers by offering exclusive, vetted travel deals to over 30 million members.

The company's expansion of AI reflects a broader industry trend of adopting AI for efficiency and improved targeting, especially as consumer spending patterns become more cautious.

feed.title: Reese’s didn’t change its chocolate because of corporate greed. It was climate change.
feed.summary: This article argues that changes in beloved food products, such as Reese's chocolate, are not due to corporate greed but rather the pervasive impact of climate change on agricultural systems. It highlights how ingredient price surges and supply instability, driven by environmental factors, force companies to subtly alter recipes or products. The author contends that consumers often misdirect their frustration towards brands, failing to recognize the deeper systemic issue of climate change affecting global food production.

feed.title: 1 Russell 2000 Stock with Impressive Fundamentals and 2 We Question

In contrast, The Marzetti Company (MZTI) and Plug Power (PLUG) are flagged as stocks to avoid, citing unexciting sales trends and commoditized products for Marzetti, and sales declines and cash burn for Plug Power.

feed.title: Clarus (CLAR) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript

feed.title: Zyrtec® joins PGA TOUR as the first official allergy relief sponsor

feed.title: DA Davidson Reduces Best Buy (BBY) Price Target Following Earnings Review
feed.summary: DA Davidson analyst Michael Baker reduced the price target for Best Buy Co., Inc. (BBY) to $78 from $85, while maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment follows a review of the company's fourth-quarter results and fiscal 2026 guidance, leading to lowered EPS estimates for fiscal years 2026 and 2027. Best Buy's Q4 revenue was $13.8 billion with an adjusted EPS of $2.61, and CEO Corie Barry noted stable market share despite a 0.8% decline in comparable sales, attributing the softness to consumer demand for electronics rather than market share loss.

feed.title: Fastest rise in global export orders in over four years
feed.summary: Global export orders experienced their fastest rise in over four years in February, primarily driven by growth in emerging markets and a strong rebound in manufacturing and services sectors. The S&P Global PMI New Export Orders rose to 51.2, marking the first expansion in nearly a year, though this data precedes recent geopolitical developments like the new conflict in the Middle East and US tariff decisions. Mainland China notably led the growth in goods export orders, while American economies showed the weakest performance.

The company projects substantial revenue and earnings growth by 2028, leading to a fair value estimate with potential upside, though investor concerns about rising costs and falling operating margins remain salient.

feed.title: JD.com posts first quarterly loss in more than three years

Despite the loss, the company's net revenues grew to 352.3 billion yuan (US$150.4 billion) in the fourth quarter and 1,309.1 billion yuan (US$187.2 billion) for the full year 2025. JD Retail, the core business, remained resilient with an operating income of 9.8 billion yuan, and CEO Sandy Xu noted the food delivery business is expanding while narrowing sequential losses.

feed.title: 'Top request from residents for many years': Port St. Lucie moves closer to landing a Costco retail store

feed.title: Amazon Web Outage Strands Customers

feed.title: Deep Dive Into Build-A-Bear Workshop Stock: Analyst Perspectives (4 Ratings)
feed.summary: Over the last three months, 4 analysts have provided ratings for Build-A-Bear Workshop (NYSE: BBW), with a current sentiment leaning bullish. The average 12-month price target is $66.25, reflecting a 1.92% increase from the previous average. Analysts highlight the company's strong net margin, impressive return on equity, and efficient asset management, despite trailing industry peers in revenue growth and having a smaller market capitalization.

feed.title: a.k.a. Brands (NYSE:AKA) Posts Quarterly Earnings Results, Misses Expectations By $0.50 EPS
feed.summary: a.k.a. Brands (NYSE:AKA) reported quarterly earnings that missed analyst expectations, posting an EPS of ($1.35) against a consensus of ($0.85). The company's revenue of $163.95 million also slightly missed estimates. Despite the earnings miss, shares remained flat, and institutional investors hold a significant portion of its stock.

feed.title: America's Car-Mart schedules fiscal Q3 2026 earnings call
feed.summary: America's Car-Mart, a major used car dealership chain, announced it will release its fiscal third quarter 2026 financial results on March 12, 2026, before the market opens. A webcast and conference call will follow at 9:00 a.m. ET to discuss the quarterly numbers. This report is significant for investors to assess consumer demand and the health of the used car market.

feed.title: Traeger: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Traeger Inc. (COOK) reported a Q4 loss of $17.2 million, or 13 cents per share, falling short of analyst expectations of 2 cents per share. Despite the loss, the barbecue grill maker exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $145.4 million against an expected $138.1 million. The company provided guidance for the current quarter, expecting revenue between $92 million and $97 million, and full-year revenue projections of $465 million to $485 million.

feed.title: Traeger: Fourth Quarter Financial Overview
feed.summary: Traeger Inc. reported a net loss of $17.2 million for its fourth quarter, translating to a loss of $0.13 per share, which was below analysts' expectations. Despite the earnings miss, the company's revenue of $145.4 million surpassed forecasts. For the upcoming quarter and full year, Traeger projects revenues of $92 million to $97 million and $465 million to $485 million, respectively.

feed.title: Texas Roadhouse Adds White Castle CEO to Board
feed.summary: Texas Roadhouse (TXRH) has appointed Lisa Ingram, CEO and Chair of White Castle System, Inc., to its board of directors on March 5, 2026. Ingram's extensive experience in the restaurant industry, marketing, and governance is expected to enhance Texas Roadhouse's strategic oversight as the company continues its domestic and international expansion. While the company demonstrates strong financial performance and unit growth, analyst Spark notes concerns regarding margin pressure, cost inflation, and a demanding valuation.

feed.title: Iowa State, Freshpet study diet's role in human-dog bond
feed.summary: Iowa State University and Freshpet are collaborating on an at-home research study to investigate how different diets impact canine health and strengthen the human-animal bond. The 10-month study involves 39 dogs from central Iowa, with owners following specific feeding protocols, collecting samples, and completing surveys. The goal is to provide comprehensive insights into the relationship between nutrition, physical well-being, and the owner-pet connection.

feed.title: McDonald's is Asking Fans to Get Real About Their First Job Skills in Exchange for Free Meals
