feed.summary: McDonald's is launching its "First Job Confessional" campaign to highlight the valuable skills gained from first jobs, often overlooked on resumes. The campaign encourages individuals to share their first job experiences and lessons learned, offering a chance to receive a $15 McDonald's gift card or have their story featured on McDonald's YouTube channel. TV personality Olandria Carthen is a spokesperson, emphasizing how her first job as a babysitter taught her essential skills.

feed.title: April 24th Options Now Available For Dollar Tree (DLTR)
feed.summary: New options contracts for Dollar Tree (DLTR) with an April 24th expiration date are now available. BNK Invest's YieldBoost formula highlights a $114.00 strike price put contract and a $119.00 strike price call contract, analyzing potential returns and probabilities for investors looking at various options strategies. These contracts offer alternatives for purchasing or selling stock, with detailed explanations of potential yields and risks.

feed.title: Live: Will Costco Smash Q2 Earnings After the Bell?
feed.summary: Costco (COST) reported its Q2 FY2026 earnings, with revenues of $69.6 billion and diluted EPS of $4.58, both slightly beating Wall Street estimates. Despite the beat and strong operational metrics like comparable sales growth of 7.4% and a 22.6% surge in e-commerce, the stock reacted with a flat to slightly lower movement. This muted reaction is attributed to Costco's premium valuation and the expectation of strong performance, indicating that the positive results were largely priced in.

feed.title: Port St. Lucie poised to bring Costco store to town
feed.summary: Port St. Lucie is finally anticipated to get a Costco store, a long-desired addition by its residents. The city is set to sell 22 acres to Costco Wholesale for the project, which will be located next to Costco's existing distribution depot in Southern Grove. A crucial vote on the land sale by the Governmental Finance Corp. is scheduled for March 9.

feed.title: Costco Earnings Beat Expectations as Membership Model Shines
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale reported fiscal second-quarter earnings of $4.58 per share, exceeding analyst expectations of $4.55 per share. This strong start to 2026 is helping the company's stock recover losses from the previous year, with the earnings beat potentially extending the current rally.

feed.title: Gap’s (NYSE:GAP) Q4 CY2025 Earnings Results: Revenue In Line With Expectations But Stock Drops
feed.summary: Gap (NYSE:GAP) announced its Q4 CY2025 earnings, with revenue of $4.24 billion meeting Wall Street expectations, marking a 2.1% year-on-year increase. However, the company's Q1 CY2026 revenue guidance of $3.51 billion fell slightly below analyst estimates, leading to a 9.5% drop in stock price. Despite hitting EPS and beating EBITDA expectations, investors reacted negatively to the forward-looking revenue projections.

feed.title: Why Grocery Outlet (GO) Shares Are Sliding Today
feed.summary: Grocery Outlet (NASDAQ:GO) shares fell sharply by 28% after the company reported disappointing fourth-quarter 2025 financial results and issued a weak forecast for 2026. Both adjusted earnings and sales missed analyst expectations, and same-store sales were flat year-on-year. The forward guidance for revenue and adjusted earnings per share significantly undershot analyst projections, leading to negative investor sentiment.

feed.title: Deutsche Bank, Wells Fargo Cut Campbell's (CPB) Price Targets as Outlook Weakens
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank and Wells Fargo have lowered their price targets for The Campbell’s Company (CPB) due to a weakening outlook, advising clients to hold or maintain an equal weight rating. Deutsche Bank revised its target from $31 to $28, and Wells Fargo reduced its target from $30 to $28, citing expectations for the company to perform at the lower end of its fiscal 2026 guidance. Concurrently, Campbell's announced the appointment of Cassandra Green as Chief Supply Chain Officer, expanding her responsibilities across the company's supply chain operations.

feed.title: DA Davidson Reduces Best Buy (BBY) Price Target Following Earnings Review
feed.summary: DA Davidson analyst Michael Baker lowered the price target for Best Buy (BBY) to $78 from $85, while maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment follows the review of the company's fourth-quarter results and 2026 guidance, with Baker reducing fiscal 2026 and 2027 EPS estimates. Best Buy reported $13.8 billion in revenue and an adjusted EPS of $2.61 for Q4 2026, with comparable sales declining 0.8%, which was within guidance.

feed.title: Costco Reports Q2 Double Beat, Comparable Sales Rise 7.4% On International Strength
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corp (NASDAQ: COST) reported a strong second quarter for fiscal 2026, surpassing analyst expectations for both revenue and earnings per share. The company saw net sales increase by 9.1% year-over-year and comparable sales rise by 7.4%, driven primarily by international strength. Despite the positive financial results, Costco's shares remained relatively flat in after-hours trading.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Target (NYSE:TGT) Stock
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has increased its price target for Target (NYSE:TGT) stock to $120.00 from $115.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating, despite potential downside from the previous close. This adjustment follows Target's strong Q4 earnings beat and a multi-year turnaround plan that includes significant investments and new store openings. While analysts acknowledge bullish catalysts, the consensus remains a "Hold" due to lingering revenue weakness and execution risks associated with the turnaround strategy.

feed.title: Gap Q4 Sales Miss Expectations After Old Navy, Athleta Fall Short
feed.summary: Gap Inc. reported fourth-quarter results that were slightly below analyst expectations, primarily due to the underperformance of its Old Navy and Athleta brands. While revenue for the period was $4.2 billion, falling short of estimates, the Gap and Banana Republic brands exceeded their comparable sales estimates.

feed.title: Best Buy to Present at the UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference on March 12
feed.summary: Best Buy (NYSE: BBY) announced that CEO Corie Barry and Chief Financial and Strategy Officer Matt Bilunas will participate in a Fireside Chat at the UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference on March 12, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. Eastern Time. A webcast of the event will be available live and after the event on the company's investor relations website. The article also includes other recent news from Best Buy, such as a quarterly cash dividend announcement and fourth-quarter results.

feed.title: CarParts.com: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: CarParts.com, Inc. (PRTS) reported a fourth-quarter loss of $11.6 million, or 17 cents per share, on revenue of $120.4 million. For the full year, the company posted a loss of $50.4 million, or 82 cents per share, with revenue totaling $547.5 million. This financial snapshot was generated by Automated Insights using data from Zacks Investment Research.

feed.title: Campbell’s appoints Green chief supply chain officer
feed.summary: The Campbell’s Co. has appointed Cassandra Green as its new chief supply chain officer, effective immediately. Green, who has been with the company for 16 years, will be responsible for customer logistics, planning, procurement, operational excellence, food safety, and quality. She reports to CEO and President Mick Beekhuizen and joins the operating committee, filling a position that had been vacant since November 2024.

feed.title: Air Force veteran to run General Mills’ global supply chain
feed.summary: General Mills has appointed Jonathan Ness as its new Chief Supply Chain Officer, effective March 16, 2026. Ness, an Air Force veteran, has been with General Mills for nearly 20 years, holding various leadership roles within the supply chain and finance sectors. He will oversee the company's global supply chain operations and report directly to Chairman and CEO Jeff Harmening.

feed.title: Yard Management: Grounds for Improvement
feed.summary: Westrock Coffee Company, a major private-label coffee and tea producer, significantly improved its yard operations by implementing a Kaleris yard management system (YMS) inherited through an acquisition. Facing challenges with increased trailer volume, manual processes, and safety concerns, the YMS provided real-time visibility, automated driver coordination, and cut trailer movement times drastically. The system's user-friendly interface and Kaleris's responsive support were key factors in Westrock's success and continued expansion.

feed.title: Shareholder Alert: Ademi LLP Investigates Claims of Securities Fraud against Grocery Outlet Holding Corp.
feed.summary: Ademi LLP has launched an investigation into Grocery Outlet Holding Corp. (NASDAQ: GO) for potential securities fraud. The investigation will examine if Grocery Outlet made inaccurate statements regarding its financial performance, business operations, and future prospects, particularly concerning issues with its store expansion strategy, new format remodels, and inventory management tools for independent operators.

feed.title: Tripadvisor reviews under the microscope: Can you still trust the stars?
feed.summary: This article examines the reliability of Tripadvisor reviews in 2026, noting that while the platform remains a powerful planning tool, users need to understand its complexities and limitations. It delves into how ratings are built, filtered, and sometimes manipulated, advising travelers on how to spot trustworthy reviews amidst increasing commercial influence and fake content. The piece concludes that Tripadvisor reviews are valuable when used critically, not as a definitive guarantee, especially for US travelers and investors watching Tripadvisor Inc. (TRIP).

feed.title: KRISPY KREME® Helps Fans 'Spring Forward' with Sweet Deal: FREE Original Glazed® Dozen on Monday with Purchase of Any Dozen
feed.summary: Krispy Kreme is offering a sweet deal to help fans "spring forward" for Daylight Saving Time. On Monday, customers can receive a free Original Glazed dozen with the purchase of any dozen at participating locations. This promotion aims to brighten the start of the week as the clocks change.

feed.title: Olaplex Holdings 10-K: $423M Revenue, $(0.01) EPS as FY25 Net Loss
feed.summary: Olaplex Holdings (OLPX) reported fiscal year net sales of $422.96 million and a net loss of $9.25 million, or $(0.01) per share. While Professional and DTC channels saw growth, this was offset by declines in specialty retail and increased operating expenses, particularly in SG&A. The company's financial results indicate stable top-line demand but show margin and profitability pressure due to elevated selling and administrative costs.

feed.title: a. Brands (NYSE: AKA) widens 2025 loss but guides higher sales for 2026
feed.summary: a.k.a. Brands Holding Corp. (NYSE: AKA) reported a widened net loss of $31.4 million in 2025 despite a 4.4% increase in net sales to $600.2 million. The company's Adjusted EBITDA declined to $19.7 million, facing higher operating expenses. For fiscal year 2026, a.k.a. Brands forecasts higher net sales between $625 million and $635 million and an improved Adjusted EBITDA of $27 million to $29 million, driven by strategic initiatives like the expansion of its Princess Polly brand with new U.S. stores and the integration of AI-driven tools.

feed.title: Travelzoo Promotes Travel Enthusiasm at World's Largest Travel Trade Show
feed.summary: Travelzoo® participated prominently at ITB Berlin, the world's largest travel trade show, showcasing its "club for travel enthusiasts" concept. The company created a multi-sensory experience with ultra-slow-motion footage of destinations, haptic materials, fragrances, and curated food, all displayed on a large LED screen. This initiative aimed to portray the deep and immersive experience of travel, inviting passionate travelers to join their club.

feed.title: Ferguson Enterprises Inc Stock (FERG) Moved Down by 3.07% on Mar 5: What Signal Does It Send?
feed.summary: Ferguson Enterprises Inc (FERG) stock declined by 3.07% on March 5th, primarily due to an earnings per share miss, ongoing weakness in the U.S. residential market impacting revenue, and a muted sales growth outlook for 2026. Other contributing factors include valuation concerns, institutional stake reductions, and negative insider selling. Despite some positive analyst ratings, these broader concerns have overshadowed more optimistic views.

feed.title: America’s Car-Mart, Inc. Schedules Third Quarter Fiscal Year 2026 Results and Conference Call
feed.summary: America's Car-Mart, Inc. (NASDAQ: CRMT) announced it will release its fiscal 2026 third-quarter financial results on Thursday, March 12, 2026, before the market opens. A webcast and conference call will be held on the same day at 9:00 a.m. ET to discuss the results. The company operates automotive dealerships in 12 states, focusing on the "Integrated Auto Sales and Finance" segment of the used car market.

feed.title: White Castle’s ‘Slider Queen’ joins Texas Roadhouse board
feed.summary: Texas Roadhouse (Nasdaq: TXRH) has appointed Lisa Ingram, the CEO and Chair of White Castle, to its Board of Directors, effective March 5, 2026. Ingram, known as the "Slider Queen," brings significant restaurant leadership and community service experience to Texas Roadhouse. Her appointment is expected to strengthen the board's expertise in restaurant operations and supply chain oversight, aligning with Texas Roadhouse's founder-inspired culture.

feed.title: Texas Roadhouse, Inc. Appoints Lisa Ingram to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Texas Roadhouse, Inc. (Nasdaq: TXRH) has announced the appointment of Lisa Ingram, CEO and Chair of White Castle System, Inc., to its Board of Directors. Ingram, a fourth-generation leader of the 105-year-old White Castle brand, brings extensive experience in the restaurant industry and a focus on people-first business practices. Jerry Morgan, Texas Roadhouse CEO, expressed excitement about her valuable contributions to the company.

feed.title: Get Up to $2,500 Back on 2025 Lexus ES as New 2026 Model Looms
feed.summary: Lexus is offering up to $2,500 in dealer cash for the 2025 Lexus ES models as a redesigned 2026 model with new powertrain options is anticipated. This incentive is available until March 31 and applies to all three 2025 ES powertrains: the ES 250, ES 350, and ES 300h. Shoppers should check with their local dealerships to determine how much of this unadvertised dealer cash will be passed on to them.

feed.title: EZPW SEC Filings - Ezcorp Inc 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K Forms
feed.summary: This page provides a comprehensive resource for investors to access EZCORP (EZPW) SEC filings, including annual reports (10-K), quarterly reports (10-Q), material event reports (8-K), and insider trading forms (Form 4). It highlights EZCORP's business as a pawn-based consumer lender and seller of pre-owned merchandise in the US and Latin America, detailing various revenue sources and operational segments. The page also features AI-powered summaries of recent filings, offering insights into insider transactions, performance metrics, and financial results.

feed.title: Burger King is testing AI headsets to both aid and monitor employees
feed.summary: Burger King is testing AI-powered headsets, named "Patty," in 500 U.S. restaurants to help employees with recipes, track inventory, and improve customer service. The system collects operational data and can alert managers to issues or provide real-time coaching for employees based on keywords used with customers. While it monitors certain interactions, Burger King states its primary purpose is coaching and enhancing hospitality, not tracking individuals.

feed.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights Liquidity Services, Remitly Global, Exponent and Sezzle
feed.summary: The Zacks Analyst Blog features four stocks—Liquidity Services, Remitly Global, Exponent, and Sezzle—poised for growth due to the strong rebound in the U.S. services sector. The ISM Services PMI reached 56.1 in February, indicating significant expansion and providing an optimistic outlook for these companies, each holding a Zacks Rank of #1 or #2. The article details the individual strengths and anticipated earnings growth of each recommended stock.

feed.title: Etsy, Inc. $ETSY Shares Bought by Elo Mutual Pension Insurance Co
feed.summary: Elo Mutual Pension Insurance Co significantly increased its stake in Etsy, acquiring an additional 21,344 shares to total 34,046 shares worth $2.26 million. This comes after Etsy authorized a $750 million share repurchase program in December, signaling management believes its shares are undervalued. Despite insider selling, institutional ownership in Etsy remains high at 99.53%.

feed.title: O'Reilly Auto Parts to pay $5.6M for denial of pregnancy accommodations to Washington workers
feed.summary: O'Reilly Auto Parts will pay $5.6 million to settle a lawsuit alleging systematic denial of pregnancy accommodations to over 50 Washington workers. The settlement, with the Washington Attorney General's Office, also mandates significant operational changes, including management training and new policies to ensure compliance with the state's Healthy Starts Act. Affected employees, like Ivannah Trinidad, will receive compensation, and the company will implement biannual compliance reports for the next four years.

feed.title: American Airlines, StubHub among stocks hit by analyst downgrades
feed.summary: Several US-listed stocks, including American Airlines, Grocery Outlet, StubHub, Edison International, and Tencent Music, experienced analyst downgrades and price target cuts. American Airlines was downgraded to Neutral with a reduced price target due to concerns about its higher per-unit costs and greater leverage compared to its peers. The downgrades have placed pressure on these companies across various sectors, with multiple price targets now close to recent trading levels.

feed.title: Is Darden Restaurants, Inc. (DRI) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bull thesis on Darden Restaurants, Inc. (DRI), highlighting its strong market position, operational efficiencies, diverse brand portfolio (including Olive Garden and LongHorn Steakhouse), and significant free cash flow generation. The author notes DRI's scale advantages in negotiating costs and its consistent same-restaurant sales growth, making it a resilient investment opportunity despite broader economic pressures. The article also mentions that while DRI shows promise, some AI stocks are perceived to offer higher returns in a shorter timeframe.

feed.title: Deckers Outdoor Corp. Opens Weak with 4.33% Gap Down Amid Market Concerns
feed.summary: Deckers Outdoor Corp. opened with a 4.33% loss, contributing to a broader negative trend for the stock, though its market capitalization is approximately USD 16.85 billion with a P/E ratio of 15.00 and a strong return on equity. The stock's one-day performance is -4.06%, compared to the S&P 500's -0.43%, and it has declined 3.07% over the past month. Technical indicators show mixed signals with bullish weekly MACD but a cautious monthly outlook, reflecting volatility in the footwear sector.

feed.title: DA Davidson raises AutoZone stock price target on comp rebound outlook
feed.summary: DA Davidson raised its price target for AutoZone Inc. (NYSE:AZO) to $4,300 from $4,100, maintaining a Buy rating, based on an anticipated rebound in comparable sales in the third quarter and expected profit improvement in the second half of 2026. This optimistic outlook comes despite various other analysts adjusting their price targets due to recent earnings results and weather-related sales impacts. The firm's new target is derived from 23 times its 2027 earnings per share forecast.

feed.title: Best Buy CEO and CFO join UBS fireside chat on March 12
feed.summary: Best Buy (NYSE: BBY) announced that its CEO, Corie Barry, and Chief Financial and Strategy Officer, Matt Bilunas, will participate in a Fireside Chat at the UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference on March 12, 2026. The event is scheduled for 8:00 a.m. Eastern Time and a webcast will be available on Best Buy's investor relations website. This appearance will allow the executives to discuss company strategy and performance with the investment community.

feed.title: Costco earns $2.0B in Q2 profit as digital sales jump 22.6%
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corporation reported strong second-quarter fiscal 2026 results, with net sales increasing 9.1% to $68.24 billion and net income rising to $2.035 billion ($4.58 diluted EPS). The company also saw a significant 22.6% jump in digitally-enabled sales, contributing to overall comparable sales growth. Costco continues to expand its global footprint, operating 924 warehouses worldwide and maintaining a robust financial position.

feed.title: Is Amazon down? Tens of thousands report problems with website
feed.summary: Tens of thousands of Amazon shoppers reported widespread problems with the website, including checkout and payment errors, with outages peaking at around 20,000 reports. Amazon apologized for the issues, which occurred two days after Facebook also experienced an outage. The problems were reported on March 5, 2026.

feed.title: Amazon: Berkshire Hathaway Q4 Trimming And Capital Lease Accounting In Focus (NASDAQ:AMZN)
feed.summary: Amazon reported strong Q4 2025 revenue growth, but its net profit margin contracted, and intensifying capital requirements could lead to negative free cash flow. Berkshire Hathaway significantly reduced its AMZN stake, citing concerns over free cash flow and valuation. The article also highlights that while AMZN's P/E ratios appear reasonable, this is partly due to aggressive capital lease accounting practices.

feed.title: Layoffs at Campbell's Soup factory near Oklahoma border top 200
feed.summary: Campbell's will lay off over 200 workers at its Paris, Texas factory. The company plans to change the plant's operational focus from soup production to other products, requiring fewer employees. The Paris factory has been in operation since 1964.

feed.title: Ross Stores reports 4Q earnings well above guidance
feed.summary: Ross Stores Inc. announced that its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 earnings significantly surpassed expectations, with a 12% increase in total sales and a 9% rise in comparable-store sales. The off-price retailer's full-year sales reached a record $22.8 billion, driven by strong holiday season performance and improved customer engagement. CEO Jim Conroy noted accelerating business momentum in the latter half of the year, despite earlier macroeconomic challenges, positioning the company well for the future.

feed.title: Etsy Director Sells $99,774 in Stock
feed.summary: Etsy Director Charles Andrew Ballard sold 1,790 shares of company stock for $99,774 on March 4th, 2026, reducing his stake by over 30%. This insider transaction may signal concerns about Etsy's future or could simply be part of his personal financial planning. Investors are advised to monitor such changes in executive ownership for insights into company outlook.

feed.title: A Look At e.l.f. Beauty (ELF) Valuation After Coty Warning Sparks Mass Beauty Sell Off
feed.summary: e.l.f. Beauty (ELF) stock dropped following a warning from rival Coty, which scrapped its fiscal 2026 outlook due to an organic sales decline, raising concerns about the mass beauty market. Despite the recent pullback, e.l.f. Beauty's long-term share price performance remains strong. The article discusses ELF's valuation, noting a "most popular narrative" suggesting it's 68% undervalued with a fair value of $251.03, while also presenting a contrasting view based on its P/E ratio, which is significantly higher than the industry average.

feed.title: Texas Roadhouse is Officially Coming to Johnstown
feed.summary: Texas Roadhouse has officially purchased property in Johnstown for a new location at the intersection of I-25 and Highway 34, following the town's significant growth in recent years. This new steakhouse will be the third Texas Roadhouse in Northern Colorado, joining existing locations in Fort Collins and Greeley. The property for the restaurant sold for $1.925 million on February 27, 2026.

feed.title: Gap (GAP) Earnings Expectation: Analysts Predict 45c Per Share
feed.summary: Analysts predict Gap Inc. (GAP) will report earnings per share of 45 cents, with the report scheduled for release after market close. The company, which operates brands like Gap, Old Navy, and Banana Republic, shows a mixed financial picture with robust profitability but high leverage and potential financial stress indicated by its Altman Z-Score. Valuation metrics suggest the stock is trading near historical highs, while insider selling activity raises a cautionary flag for potential investors.

feed.title: American Century Companies Boosts Stake in Regency Centers
feed.summary: American Century Companies Inc. has increased its stake in Regency Centers Corporation by 10.8% in the third quarter of 2025, bringing its total ownership to 957,588 shares valued at approximately $69.8 million. This move signals confidence in Regency Centers, a major owner of grocery-anchored shopping centers, and reflects growing investor interest in the retail real estate sector post-pandemic. The investment highlights the continued appeal of well-located, essential retail properties in dense urban markets.

feed.title: Altria Continues to Lead in US Combustibles, Though It Lags Peers in Reduced-Risk Products
feed.summary: Altria (MO) maintains its dominant position in the U.S. combustible cigarette market, primarily thanks to its Marlboro brand which holds a 40% market share. Despite this, the company faces challenges as the U.S. market relies on price increases to offset volume declines. The article indicates that Altria lags its peers in the reduced-risk product category.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Announces an Investigation Against CarMax, Inc. (KMX) and Encourages Stockholders to Learn More About the Investigation
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has announced an investigation into potential corporate wrongdoing by CarMax, Inc. (NYSE:KMX) and its officers/directors. The firm is encouraging stockholders who purchased CarMax securities prior to June 20, 2025, and still hold them, to contact them to learn more and assist with the investigation. The firm specializes in securities fraud class actions and shareholder derivative suits, representing investors on a contingency fee basis.

feed.title: Costco Earnings Due. These Retailers Retreat On Results; One Surges.
feed.summary: Retail stocks saw mixed performance on Thursday following a series of earnings reports. While Costco Wholesale slid ahead of its earnings release and Victoria's Secret and BJ's Wholesale retreated, Burlington surged into a buy zone. FactSet anticipates Costco's fiscal second-quarter earnings to increase by 13% to $4.55 per share.

feed.title: American Express Reimagines the Airport Lounge Experience
feed.summary: American Express has launched "Sidecar by the Centurion Lounge" at Harry Reid International Airport in Las Vegas, a new concept offering an intimate, speakeasy-inspired cocktail bar experience for travelers with limited time before flights. This 33-seat lounge focuses on curated small plates and drinks from award-winning chefs and beverage professionals, rather than the larger, traditional lounge amenities. The initiative provides an elevated, quick-stop alternative for eligible Amex cardholders, complementing their existing Centurion Lounges, with plans for expansion to other airports.

feed.title: Citigroup Maintains 'Buy' Rating, Raises Price Target for Darden Restaurants (DRI) | DRI Stock News
feed.summary: Citigroup has reiterated its 'Buy' rating for Darden Restaurants (DRI) and increased its price target slightly from $234 to $235, as analyzed by Jon Tower. This adjustment comes amidst various recent analyst rating changes for DRI, with some upgrading and others downgrading the stock. Darden Restaurants, the largest full-service restaurant operator in the US, maintains a diverse portfolio of 11 brands, and analysts generally forecast an "Outperform" status for the company, with an average target price of $220.79.

feed.title: Darden Restaurants (NYSE:DRI) Given New $235.00 Price Target at Citigroup
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Darden Restaurants (NYSE:DRI) to $235.00, reiterating a "buy" rating and suggesting a 13.97% upside. This adjustment follows Darden's mixed quarterly results, where EPS slightly missed expectations but revenue surpassed forecasts, and the company issued strong FY2026 guidance. Despite some insider selling, institutional investors hold a significant majority of the stock, reflecting overall confidence in the restaurant operator.

feed.title: WHOOP Selects Viant as Strategic Ad Platform
feed.summary: Viant Technology Inc. has announced a multi-year strategic partnership with WHOOP, a human performance company. Viant will serve as WHOOP's DSP of Record, utilizing AI-powered programmatic advertising and household-level CTV activation to drive measurable growth and connect media exposure to business results. This collaboration highlights Viant's leadership in CTV advertising and WHOOP's commitment to data-driven marketing strategies for reaching new customers.

feed.title: These 40 small towns are vying for the 'America's Favorite' crown
feed.summary: Parade and Steller have announced the 40 finalists for "America's Favorite Small Town" competition, selected through nationwide entries and public voting. These towns, including places like Lodi, California, and Fredericksburg, Texas, have populations under 250,000 and represent the diverse character of the U.S. Regional winners will be declared on June 1, 2026, and the national winner on June 12, 2026, who will receive official branding and a grand prize activation at their town's July 4th celebration.

feed.title: Distribution Solutions Group Posts Flat Sales Growth, Narrows Loss
feed.summary: Distribution Solutions Group (DSG) reported nearly flat year-over-year sales growth in its latest quarter, with revenue reaching $481.6 million, and managed to narrow its loss to $6.4 million. Despite this, the company's operating income significantly dropped by over 60% due to factors like non-cash intangible amortization and various non-recurring costs. CEO Bryan King acknowledged the Q4 margin did not meet expectations, attributing it to product mix shifts and increased employee-related costs, but maintained confidence in the company's long-term strategy.

feed.title: NPK's 2025 Earnings Fall Y/Y Due to Housewares Unit Weakness
feed.summary: National Presto Industries, Inc. (NPK) saw its 2025 earnings per share drop to $4.63 from $5.82 in 2024, despite a 29.7% rise in net sales, primarily driven by its Defense segment. This decline in profitability was attributed to the Housewares/Small Appliance segment's struggles due to tariff-related pressures, resulting in decreased revenue and an operating loss. The company's management highlighted tariffs as a major factor affecting performance and outlook, and while a regular dividend was announced, no special dividend will be paid due to cash allocation for the Defense segment's backlog.

feed.title: Massimo Group (NASDAQ: MAMO) Secures Authorization for Core Product Placement Across 1,000 U.S. Retail Locations
feed.summary: Massimo Group (NASDAQ: MAMO) has secured authorization from a national rural lifestyle retail partner for the placement of one of its core products across 1,000 U.S. retail locations. This initial rollout includes approximately 2,400 units, supported by both physical stores and an online platform, creating an omnichannel sales approach. The agreement also provides a scalable pathway for potential expansion to the retailer's roughly 2,200 U.S. locations based on performance.

feed.title: Massimo Group secures placement in 1,000 retail locations By Investing.com
feed.summary: Massimo Group (NASDAQ:MAMO) has announced that one of its products will be placed in 1,000 U.S. retail locations of a national rural lifestyle retailer, representing a significant portion of the retailer's 2,200 U.S. stores. This expansion follows Massimo reporting $70.9 million in revenue over the past year, and comes amid other developments like a proposed acquisition of AI firm FST Development Company Limited and the launch of the Sentinel 770 HVAC UTV. InvestingPro suggests the stock is currently undervalued.

feed.title: Five Below’s Newest Home Find Is a Gorgeous Addition to Any Room
feed.summary: Five Below is offering a new flameless candle warmer for just $20, providing an affordable and safe way to enjoy home fragrances. This device melts wax evenly and extends the scent's longevity without smoke or soot, making it a popular choice for those seeking budget-friendly and safer home ambiance solutions. It's especially appealing to apartment dwellers, parents, and pet owners looking for alternatives to open flames.

feed.title: T-Mobile $200 Gift Card Class Action Lawsuit: Who Could Be Eligible?
feed.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against T-Mobile, alleging that the company failed to provide promotional $200 gift cards as promised for new phone lines. The plaintiff, Purya Ghrabeti, claims T-Mobile induced customers with nonexistent promotions, leading to accusations of false advertising and deceptive business practices. The lawsuit seeks to represent California consumers affected by these alleged unfulfilled financial incentives and requests a jury trial for restitution and other relief.

feed.title: GXO Logistics to lay off 100+ workers as West Jefferson facility closes
feed.summary: GXO Logistics is set to close its West Jefferson facility, resulting in over 100 layoffs. This closure is part of a series of layoffs the company has implemented in recent years. The article also references other recent job cuts in the Columbus area.

feed.title: Technical Reactions to POOL Trends in Macro Strategies
feed.summary: Pool Corporation (NASDAQ: POOL) is showing weak sentiment across all horizons, supporting a short-term bias according to AI models. The analysis provides institutional trading strategies for different risk profiles, including position trading, momentum breakout, and risk hedging, alongside multi-timeframe signal analysis. Key support and resistance levels are identified, with elevated downside risk noted due to the absence of long-term support signals.

feed.title: Fastenal Reports a More than 13% Jump in February Sales
feed.summary: Fastenal announced a 13.3% increase in February sales compared to the previous year, reaching $710.6 million. The growth was observed across all geographies, end markets, and customer segments, with significant jumps in heavy manufacturing, non-residential construction, OEM, and MRO categories. This follows a double-digit year-over-year increase in January and an 11.1% increase in its latest fiscal quarter.

feed.title: Target opens 30 stores this year, including 'food-forward' prototype
feed.summary: Target plans to open 30 new stores this year, reaching a total of 2,000 locations. This expansion is part of a $5 billion investment aimed at reversing recent quarterly sales declines. One of the new store formats will include a "food-forward" prototype.

feed.title: Walmart to add digital shelf labels to all US stores. What to know in Wisconsin
feed.summary: Walmart plans to implement digital shelf labels in all U.S. stores in 2026, building on a rollout that began in 2024. These new digital price tags aim to ensure accurate pricing and save employees time by eliminating manual price changes. Wisconsin, with its 97 Walmart stores, is among the states where shoppers will see this change, which does not involve microphones or cameras.

feed.title: A US company you've never heard of lost more than $16 million because of Trump
feed.summary: Weyco Group Inc., a Wisconsin-based shoe company, lost over $16 million due to tariffs imposed by former President Trump, leading to a 24 percent drop in profits. The company passed some costs to consumers and rerouted production overseas. Weyco Group Inc. has since sued the Trump administration to recoup its losses, especially after the Supreme Court ruled that Trump's use of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to justify tariffs was an overreach.

feed.title: Cintas Nears Acquisition of Uniform Maker UniFirst
feed.summary: Cintas Corp. is reportedly in advanced talks to acquire UniFirst Corp., a rival uniform maker, potentially concluding a long-standing pursuit. UniFirst shares saw a significant jump of up to 17% on the news. An announcement regarding the deal could come as early as next week, following Cintas's resubmission of a $275-a-share takeover offer in December.

feed.title: Kroger is closing Little Clinic healthcare locations in Indiana
feed.summary: Kroger is closing several of its in-store Little Clinic healthcare locations in Indiana, with some already shut down and others pending closure. These closures are part of a larger company-wide move, affecting locations in other states like Georgia and Ohio, and come amidst anticipated announcements regarding overall Kroger grocery store closures. Despite the physical clinic shutdowns, Kroger states that nearby locations will remain open and virtual appointments will be available for patients.

feed.title: Distribution Solutions (DSGR) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
feed.summary: Distribution Solutions (DSGR) stock fell 25.3% after reporting Q4 2025 results that missed Wall Street's revenue and profit expectations. The company's revenue was flat year-over-year, and adjusted earnings per share were significantly below estimates, largely due to a sharp decline in operating margin. This significant market reaction indicates a major impact on the perception of the business, with the stock now trading 31.5% below its 52-week high.

feed.title: Why Victoria's Secret (VSCO) Stock Is Down Today
feed.summary: Victoria's Secret (VSCO) stock fell by 11.8% after its fourth-quarter earnings report, despite beating revenue and earnings expectations. The decline was primarily driven by a significant year-over-year decrease in operating margin, which suggested rising expenses and raised investor concerns about future profitability. This profitability concern outweighed an otherwise optimistic sales forecast for the next quarter.

feed.title: Quince Antitrust Row Over Ugg IP Suits Must Navigate Free Speech
feed.summary: Quince is suing Ugg owner Deckers Outdoor Corp. for alleged antitrust violations, claiming the company filed "sham" trademark lawsuits to monopolize the sheepskin footwear market. The case presents a constitutional challenge, as Quince must prove antitrust violations while navigating the First Amendment protections of companies' rights to litigate. The legal battle highlights the tension between intellectual property enforcement and competitive practices in the billion-dollar slipper market.

feed.title: Trade Tracker: Jenny Harrington buys Best Buy and Karen Firestone buys more Novo Nordisk
feed.summary: Jenny Harrington, CEO of Gilman Hill Asset Management, has purchased Best Buy (BBY) stock. Karen Firestone, Executive Chairman of Aureus Asset Management, has increased her holdings in Novo Nordisk (NVO). These portfolio moves were shared during CNBC's "Halftime Report".

feed.title: Ashish Arora, Cricut CEO, sells $313k in CRCT stock
feed.summary: Cricut (NASDAQ:CRCT) CEO Ashish Arora sold 73,458 shares of Class A Common Stock on March 2nd and 3rd, 2026, totaling $313,853. These sales were executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. The company recently surpassed Q4 2025 earnings expectations with an EPS of $0.04 and revenue of $203.6 million, though it experienced a year-over-year revenue decline in its Product segment.

feed.title: BJ's Wholesale Club CEO Flags 'Dynamic Environment' Of Tariffs, Cautious Consumers
feed.summary: BJ’s Wholesale Club (NYSE: BJ) shares fell despite beating Q4 earnings and revenue estimates, as the company's operating income was softer and its fiscal 2026 outlook came in below Wall Street expectations. CEO Bob Eddy highlighted a dynamic environment with cautious consumers, tariff-related uncertainties, and macroeconomic volatility. The company forecasts fiscal 2026 adjusted EPS below estimates and plans capital expenditures for expansion and network upgrades.

feed.title: Texas Roadhouse vs. Outback Steakhouse: the clear winner for best and most affordable Bone-in Ribeye
feed.summary: This article compares the Bone-in Ribeye offerings at Texas Roadhouse and Outback Steakhouse, focusing on affordability and taste. While both serve USDA Choice cuts, Texas Roadhouse generally provides a better value and often higher-rated steak, despite having a higher calorie count for its 20-ounce serving. Outback is praised for its consistent flavor and seasoning, but Texas Roadhouse marginally wins the taste test for its rich, succulent, and more beefy flavor.

feed.title: Honey chipotle BBQ to horchata coffee: El Pollo Loco tests bold menu
feed.summary: El Pollo Loco (NASDAQ: LOCO) is currently testing five new menu items, including Honey Chipotle BBQ meals, Loco Tenders, Loaded Quesadillas, a Crispy Grilled Chicken Sandwich, and Horchata coffee drinks, across approximately 60 restaurants in California and Utah until late April 2026. This initiative is part of the company's 2026 focus on culinary innovation, guest variety, and iterative feedback, with plans for eight more menu tests later in the year. The tests aim to explore new flavor directions and expand the brand's offerings based on evolving customer cravings.

feed.title: TRNR Updates FAQ'S Following Full Loan Recovery and Successful Legal Settlement with Sportstech
feed.summary: Interactive Strength Inc. (Nasdaq:TRNR) announced it has updated its FAQs following a full loan recovery and a successful legal settlement with Sportstech. The company, known for its Wattbike, CLMBR, and FORME fitness brands, urges shareholders to review the new information. TRNR is also the pending acquirer of Ergatta.

feed.title: Teledyne e2v Introduces Perciva™ 5D Camera: Occlusion-free 3D Vision for Industrial, Retail, and Robotic Imaging
feed.summary: Teledyne e2v has launched the Perciva™ 5D camera, an innovative imaging solution providing cost-effective and reliable short-range 3D vision for industrial, retail, and robotic applications. The camera uses unique Angular Sensitive Pixel technology and a Neural Processing Unit to generate 2D and 3D data from a single CMOS sensor without optical occlusion, even in ambient light. It features plug-and-play integration and is suitable for various challenging environments, including robotics and self-checkout systems.

feed.title: O’Reilly Auto Parts to pay $5.6M for employee discrimination
feed.summary: O’Reilly Auto Enterprises will pay $5.6 million in a consent decree with Washington state for systematically denying pregnancy and nursing accommodations to its employees. The agreement, which follows a lawsuit alleging violations of state law and retaliation against workers, requires O’Reilly to compensate over 50 harmed employees and implement new policies and training. Although the company denies discrimination, it agreed to the settlement to focus on team member safety and wellness training.

feed.title: Is Coca-Cola's Digital Transformation a Catalyst for Margin Expansion?
feed.summary: The article discusses how Coca-Cola's digital transformation initiatives, including integrating digital tools across marketing, customer engagement, and supply-chain operations, are expected to drive efficiency and long-term margin expansion. It highlights similar efforts by peers like PepsiCo and Monster Beverage and notes that Coca-Cola's stock has outperformed its industry peers, with positive earnings growth projections. The company currently holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

feed.title: Barco Joins The Coca-cola Company As Official Co-Presenting Sponsor Of CinemaCon 2026
feed.summary: Barco has announced its partnership with The Coca-Cola Company as an official co-presenting sponsor of CinemaCon 2026, marking the first time the convention will have two co-presenting sponsors since rebranding in 2011. This expanded partnership highlights Barco's commitment to the global theatrical experience, with the company also sponsoring a new Trade Show Networking Reception and showcasing its laser projection technology. CinemaCon, hosted by Cinema United, will take place from April 13-16 at Caesars Palace in Las Vegas.

feed.title: Is Darden Restaurants, Inc. (DRI) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Darden Restaurants, Inc. (DRI), highlighting its operational philosophy, scale advantages, diverse brand portfolio, and strong free cash flow generation. The company, which owns brands like Olive Garden and LongHorn Steakhouse, is seen as a resilient investment in the casual dining space, capable of navigating economic challenges and providing consistent shareholder returns. Despite its potential, the article notes that DRI is not among the most popular stocks with hedge funds.

feed.title: Is Darden Restaurants, Inc. (DRI) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Darden Restaurants, Inc. (DRI), highlighting its operational philosophy, scale advantages, diversified portfolio of casual dining brands, and strong free cash flow generation. The company's ability to negotiate costs, maintain customer loyalty, and deliver consistent profitability positions it as a resilient investment opportunity despite macroeconomic headwinds.

feed.title: Dollar General slides 4.5% as traders de-risk ahead of earnings and technicals cool off
feed.summary: Dollar General (DG) stock dropped 4.5% as traders de-risk before its upcoming quarterly earnings release on March 12, 2026. Technical indicators had shown the stock in "overbought" conditions, contributing to the profit-taking. Insider trading data reveals significant sales, while institutional investors show mixed activity ahead of the earnings call.

feed.title: Kroger turns in modest results as it eyes growth under new CEO
feed.summary: Kroger's new CEO, Greg Foran, is prioritizing improving the in-store experience, cutting prices, and accelerating sales growth. The company reported a 2.4% identical-store sales increase (excluding fuel) in Q4 2025, driven by digital, pharmacy, and fresh sales, with overall sales reaching $34.7 billion. Foran emphasized that the focus is on executing the existing strategy better and faster, aiming for 1% to 2% identical sales growth without fuel in 2026, and achieving e-commerce profitability.

feed.title: New 5D camera lets robots and self-checkouts see in 3D
feed.summary: Teledyne e2v has launched the Perciva™ 5D camera, an innovative imaging solution designed to provide cost-effective, reliable, and easy-to-integrate high-quality short-range 3D vision. This new camera leverages a unique Angular Sensitive Pixel technology and an on-board Neural Processing Unit to generate both 2D and 3D data from a single CMOS sensor, free from optical occlusion. It's ideal for robotics, retail self-checkout systems, and industrial process monitoring, operating efficiently in various lighting conditions without requiring an external NIR source.

feed.title: Tapestry Inc (TPR, US8760301072): Coach owner navigates luxury slowdown and integration risks into 2026
feed.summary: Tapestry Inc. (TPR), owner of Coach, Kate Spade, and Stuart Weitzman, is balancing a challenging global luxury market with its planned acquisition of Capri Holdings. The company's performance is sensitive to US consumer demand, Federal Reserve policy, and the aspirational luxury market. The acquisition is a significant strategic move, promising diversification and increased scale, but also presents integration and regulatory risks.

feed.title: Liquidity Mapping Around (OXM) Price Events
feed.summary: This article from Stock Traders Daily discusses liquidity mapping around price events for Oxford Industries Inc. (OXM). It identifies near-term weak sentiment, potential for long-term weakness resumption, and highlights an exceptional 51.9:1 risk-reward short setup. The piece also provides three distinct AI-generated trading strategies (Position, Momentum Breakout, Risk Hedging) and multi-timeframe signal analysis for OXM.

feed.title: A.k.a. Brands: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: A.k.a. Brands Holding Corp. reported a fourth-quarter loss of $14.5 million, or $1.35 per share, with adjusted losses of 80 cents per share. The company posted revenue of $164 million for the period and expects current quarter revenue between $130 million and $132 million, with full-year revenue projected at $625 million to $635 million. Shares have decreased almost 4% year-to-date and 29% over the last 12 months.

feed.title: Victoria’s Secret Sales Grow by 8% in Q4
feed.summary: Victoria's Secret & Co. exceeded sales and profit expectations in Q4 2025, with an 8% increase in sales to $2.3 billion, driven by a strategic focus on core businesses like bras and Pink, and growth in its beauty segment. CEO Hillary Super highlighted the success of her four-part plan to rebuild the brand, leading to annual growth in the bra category and renewed relevance for Pink. The company anticipates continued growth in 2026, projecting sales between $6.9 billion and $7 billion, while also reviewing non-core assets like DailyLook for divestiture.

feed.title: Victoria’s Secret & Co. ends Adore Me subscription offering
feed.summary: Victoria’s Secret & Co. has ended the intimates subscription offering for its Adore Me business, converting it to a loyalty program. The company is also performing a strategic review of DailyLook, an online personal styling service acquired through the Adore Me transaction. This move follows the retailer’s fourth-quarter results, which showed net sales growth and a focus on core brands.

feed.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Reminds Investors with Losses on their Investment in Bath & Body Works, Inc. of Class Action Lawsuit and Upcoming Deadlines - BBWI
feed.summary: Pomerantz LLP has announced a class action lawsuit against Bath & Body Works, Inc. (BBWI) following significant stock price drops. The lawsuit stems from the company reporting substantial declines in earnings and revenue, and repeatedly cutting its full-year guidance in recent financial reports. Investors who suffered losses on their Bath & Body Works securities purchased during the Class Period have until March 16, 2026, to seek appointment as Lead Plaintiff.

feed.title: Jack in the Box centralizes inventory at all stores
feed.summary: Jack in the Box has implemented Restaurant365's centralized inventory solution across all 2,128 of its corporate and franchisee locations. This move aims to standardize inventory management, simplify its technology environment, and provide real-time financial and operational visibility to improve profitability and performance. The deployment follows other technology modernizations, including a unified commerce platform and AI solutions for customer feedback analysis and location intelligence.

feed.title: Frosted animal cookies, but huggable: inside Build-A-Bear’s new collection
feed.summary: Build-A-Bear (NYSE:BBW) launched its new Frosted Animal Cookie Collection on March 5, 2026, featuring plush toys inspired by pink-and-white frosted animal cookies. The collection includes Frosted Animal Cookie Giraffe and Camel, three Mini Beans, a cookie-inspired scent, and themed clothing and accessories, available in stores and online. This launch aligns with Build-A-Bear's "The Stuff You Love" platform, emphasizing nostalgia and personalized, experiential retail.

feed.title: Pricesmart Inc (PSMT) Shares Down 3.53% on Mar 5
feed.summary: Pricesmart Inc (PSMT) shares fell 3.53% on March 5, trading at $147.65, which is 6.56% below its 52-week high. Analysts have an average target price of $139.14, implying a 5.77% downside, with a consensus "Outperform" rating. GuruFocus estimates a fair value (GF Value) of $111.59, suggesting a larger downside of 24.42%.

feed.title: Brilliant Earth Releases Fifth Annual Mission Report, Celebrating Two Decades of Transforming the Jewelry Industry
feed.summary: Brilliant Earth, a leader in ethically sourced fine jewelry, released its fifth annual Mission Report, highlighting two decades of impact and future initiatives. The report showcases the company's progress across Transparency, Sustainability, Compassion, and Inclusion, detailing achievements like its #1 ranking in sustainable jewelry, increased Fairmined gold purchases, and over $2 million committed to non-profits. Brilliant Earth remains a female-founded and led company, recognized for its workplace culture and leadership in responsible business practices.

feed.title: National Vision Holdings (EYE): Barclays Raises Price Target to $38 | EYE Stock News
feed.summary: Barclays has reiterated its "Overweight" rating for National Vision Holdings (EYE) and increased its price target from $32 to $38, indicating an 18.75% rise. This adjustment reflects sustained confidence in the company's growth potential. Other analysts have also recently issued positive updates for EYE, with an average target price of $34.45 from 11 analysts, suggesting a 20.34% upside from the current price.

feed.title: Popular Fast Food Chain Closing Locations, Including Indiana
feed.summary: FAT Brands Inc., the California-based restaurant conglomerate that owns Fazoli’s, has filed for Chapter 11 reorganization bankruptcy, leading to the closure of some Fazoli's locations, including one in Evansville, Indiana. Despite the bankruptcy and previous legal issues faced by CEO Andy Wiederhorn, the company states its focus remains on customer service and supporting its franchise partners. Twenty-eight Fazoli's locations still operate in Indiana, with more restaurant and retail closures expected in the state in 2026.

feed.title: American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE:AEO) Shares Gap Down Following Analyst Downgrade
feed.summary: American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE:AEO) shares gapped down by about 11.8% after Telsey Advisory Group lowered its price target to $25 and issued a "market perform" rating, despite the company beating Q4 earnings estimates. While AEO reported strong Q4 results and announced a dividend, some analysts are concerned about potential margin pressure, leading to a consensus "Hold" rating with an average target of $22.18. Institutional ownership is high, though insiders have sold a significant number of shares recently.

feed.title: Rebecca Minkoff Expands Partnership with QVC, Introducing Debut Footwear Line and Exclusive RM Studio Spring Collection
feed.summary: Rebecca Minkoff is expanding her partnership with QVC by launching her debut footwear line and a new RM Studio x Rebecca Minkoff spring collection. The new collection features trend-forward apparel in inclusive sizing and a variety of footwear styles, building on the success of previous collaborations. This expansion aims to offer QVC customers a complete head-to-toe look, further growing the Rebecca Minkoff brand through this strategic collaboration.

feed.title: Red Robin Gourmet Burgers Reports Results for the Fiscal First Quarter Ended April 15, 2012
feed.summary: Red Robin Gourmet Burgers, Inc. (NASDAQ: RRGB) reported mixed financial results for the fiscal first quarter ended April 15, 2012. While earnings per diluted share increased by 22.4% to $0.71 and total revenues rose 4.4% to $299.5 million, company-owned comparable restaurant net sales only increased by 0.5% due to a 3.6% decrease in guest counts. The company opened four new restaurants during the quarter and provided an updated outlook for fiscal year 2012, projecting up to 1% growth in comparable restaurant net sales.

feed.title: Bath & Body Works, Inc. (BBWI) Shareholders Who Lost Money Have Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz announced that shareholders of Bath & Body Works, Inc. (BBWI) who experienced losses have an opportunity to lead a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The complaint alleges that the company and its executives made materially misleading statements between June 4, 2024, and November 19, 2025, regarding its growth strategies, relying on collaborations to obscure weak financial results. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to contact the firm before the lead plaintiff deadline of March 16, 2026.

feed.title: Lifetime Brands (LCUT) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Lifetime Brands (LCUT) is expected to beat earnings estimates for the quarter ended December 2025, despite projections of a year-over-year decline in earnings and revenues. This positive outlook is supported by a 2.94% increase in the consensus EPS estimate over the last 30 days and a strong Zacks Earnings ESP of +37.93% combined with a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), indicating a high probability of exceeding expectations. Investors are advised to consider these factors, along with the company's past surprise history, before making investment decisions.

feed.title: Walmart adds digital shelf labels to all US stores. What to know.
feed.summary: Walmart is expanding digital shelf labels to all U.S. stores within the next year, moving away from paper price tags to a centralized digital system. This change aims to improve efficiency by allowing price adjustments to be made within minutes and helping employees locate items for stocking and online orders more quickly. The digital labels do not include cameras or microphones.

feed.title: After reaching the higher Royal Caribbean loyalty levels, these are the 4 best cruises to maximize Crown & Anchor loyalty benefits
feed.summary: This article highlights how Royal Caribbean values its top-tier Crown & Anchor loyalty members and suggests four types of cruises to maximize loyalty benefits. These include President's Cruises for superfans, longer repositioning sailings to earn more points, cruises visiting private islands to utilize onboard perks, and strategic use of free Pinnacle Club cruises on new Caribbean ships. The author, a Pinnacle Club member, shares personal insights on leveraging these benefits.

feed.title: As Last Mile Delivery Shifts Toward Orchestration and AI, OneRail Recognized in the Gartner® Market Guide
feed.summary: OneRail, a provider of AI-native last mile fulfillment and delivery orchestration solutions, has been recognized for the third consecutive time in the 2025 Gartner® Market Guide for Last Mile Delivery Technology Solutions. This inclusion highlights OneRail's platform, OmniPoint®, which helps retailers and wholesale distributors manage complex delivery networks, make data-driven decisions, and improve performance. The recognition comes as the last mile delivery market increasingly focuses on orchestration and AI to meet evolving customer expectations and operational demands.

feed.title: Fastenal stock sees BofA reiterate Buy on February sales strength
feed.summary: BofA Securities reiterated a Buy rating and a $48.00 price target for Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) after the company reported strong February sales performance, with daily sales growth up 13.3% year-over-year. This acceleration in sales, coupled with a 27% return over the past year and a 34-year dividend streak, highlights the company's strong momentum despite some analysts noting potential overvaluation and recent earnings missing some projections. Other analysts from Bernstein, UBS, and Morgan Stanley offer varying outlooks on the stock based on recent financial reports and sales figures.

feed.title: Dollar General Corp (DG) Stock Price Down 3.32% on Mar 5
feed.summary: Dollar General Corp's (DG) stock price dropped 3.32% on March 5th, trading at $146.57, which is 7.37% below its 52-week high but significantly above its 52-week low. Wall Street analysts maintain an "Outperform" rating with an average target price of $147.28, suggesting a slight upside. However, GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $138.73, indicating a potential downside from its current price.

feed.title: Why Is Chipotle (CMG) Down 8.1% Since Last Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG) shares have fallen 8.1% since its last earnings report, underperforming the S&P 500 despite beating Q4 2025 earnings and revenue estimates. While the company saw growth in new restaurant openings and digital sales, comparable restaurant sales slightly declined. Analysts are showing a downward trend in estimate revisions, leading to a Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell) for CMG.

feed.title: Stocks making the biggest moves after hours: Costco, Marvell Technology, Gap & more
feed.summary: This article details the after-hours stock performance of several companies following their earnings reports. Costco dipped slightly despite beating analyst expectations, while Marvell Technology surged due to strong quarterly results driven by AI demand. Gap's stock slid nearly 8% after its earnings slightly missed forecasts, and Samsara jumped over 11% on rosy guidance, reporting its use of AI for workflow automation.

feed.title: Barco Joins Coca-Cola as Co-Presenting Sponsor of CinemaCon 2026
feed.summary: Barco has joined The Coca-Cola Company as a co-presenting sponsor for CinemaCon 2026, marking the first time the global theatrical convention will have two presenting partners. This partnership highlights Barco's commitment to the cinema industry, showcasing its laser projection technology and emphasizing collaboration among filmmakers, studios, exhibitors, and technology partners. CinemaCon 2026, organized by Cinema United, will take place from April 13-16 at Caesars Palace in Las Vegas.

feed.title: Cracker Barrel sees slow improvement despite continued traffic slump in Q2
feed.summary: Cracker Barrel is showing slow signs of improvement despite an ongoing traffic slump in Q2, reporting a 7.1% decline in same-store sales but improved traffic and guest scores in January. The company is focusing on heritage, food, and value perception improvements after abandoning controversial brand modernization plans. Strategies include reintroducing menu favorites, adding more breakfast options, implementing barbell pricing, and undertaking cost-saving measures.

feed.title: As Last Mile Delivery Shifts Toward Orchestration and AI, OneRail Recognized in the Gartner® Market Guide
feed.summary: OneRail, a leading final mile delivery orchestration platform, has been recognized in the 2024 Gartner Market Guide for Last-Mile Delivery Technology. The report emphasizes the growing trend of leveraging AI for advanced orchestration, highlighting OneRail's capabilities in unifying disparate systems, optimizing delivery decisions, and providing end-to-end visibility. This recognition underscores OneRail's role in helping enterprises achieve greater efficiency, customer satisfaction, and scalability in their last-mile operations.

feed.title: Darden invests $3.5M in new Seasons 52 location in Winter Garden
feed.summary: Darden Restaurants is investing $3.5 million to open a new Seasons 52 location in Winter Garden, Florida, on the site of a former Romano's Macaroni Grill. A recently issued construction permit revealed the cost for the new restaurant. This development is part of a larger shopping center that attracts millions of visitors annually.

feed.title: Gap Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal 2025 Results; Provides Fiscal 2026 Outlook
feed.summary: Gap Inc. reported strong financial results for its fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2025, with net sales growing 2% and comparable sales up 3%, marking its second consecutive year of topline growth. The company achieved a full-year operating income of $1.1 billion and generated $1.3 billion in operating cash flow, leading to the approval of a new $1 billion share repurchase authorization. Gap Inc. also provided its fiscal 2026 outlook, expecting continued net sales growth and operating margin expansion, while increasing its first-quarter dividend.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty (ULTA) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: Can the Stock Move Higher?
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty (ULTA) is expected to report its earnings for the quarter ended January 2026 on March 12, with analysts anticipating a year-over-year decline in earnings but higher revenues. The company has a Zacks Earnings ESP of +11.98% and a Zacks Rank #3, suggesting a strong likelihood of an earnings beat. Historically, Ulta has consistently surpassed EPS estimates, beating consensus in all of the last four quarters.

feed.title: CVS Health partners with Google; launching health tech subsidiary
feed.summary: CVS Health is partnering with Google Cloud to launch Health100, a new health technology services subsidiary. This collaboration aims to revolutionize healthcare experiences, enhance consumer engagement, and support better health outcomes by leveraging Google's AI technologies, including Gemini models. Health100 will provide an integrated platform designed to offer real-time omnichannel support and connect consumers with pharmacist-led care management, with an initial launch planned for 2026.

feed.title: Pilot Turns to NCR Voyix in a POS Modernization Move
feed.summary: Pilot Travel Centers will adopt point-of-sale technology from NCR Voyix Corp. across all its 900+ locations under a new five-year exclusive agreement. This modernization effort aims to simplify Pilot's tech infrastructure and enhance performance, uptime, and security, leveraging NCR Voyix's Commerce Platform. The deal expands an existing partnership and follows NCR Voyix's recent agreement to upgrade POS systems for 7-Eleven Philippines.

feed.title: Victoria's Secret & Co. Stock Falls 14% Over Lower Earnings In Q4
feed.summary: Victoria's Secret & Co. (VSCO) stock dropped approximately 14% following the announcement of its fourth-quarter financial results. The company reported a decline in earnings to $183.63 million, or $2.14 per share, compared to $193.41 million, or $2.33 per share, in the previous year. Despite the earnings dip, revenue for the period increased to $2.269 billion from $2.105 billion.

feed.title: Birkenstock Holding PLC (NYSE:BIRK) Receives Average Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" from Analysts
feed.summary: Birkenstock Holding PLC (NYSE:BIRK) has been given a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating by nineteen analysts, with an average 12-month target price of $60.12. The company recently reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings of $0.60 EPS against an anticipated $0.40, with revenues up 15.5% year-over-year, and has issued strong FY2026 guidance. Institutional and hedge fund ownership in BIRK has significantly increased, indicating growing investor interest.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Olaplex Holdings Q4 2025 results show mixed performance
feed.summary: Olaplex Holdings (OLPX) reported mixed Q4 2025 earnings, with an EPS of $0.01 significantly beating forecasts but revenue falling slightly short at $105.1 million. Despite a 4.3% year-over-year revenue growth driven by professional and direct-to-consumer channels, the stock dropped 17.98% in pre-market trading due to the revenue miss and increased operational expenses impacting margins. The company aims for continued transformation in 2026, focusing on energizing hero products, science-based innovation, and expanding its go-to-market model.

feed.title: Marcus eyes new theater at Pabst Farms next to Oconomowoc Fleet Farm
feed.summary: Marcus Corp. is planning a new theater at Pabst Farms in Oconomowoc, on a 16.5-acre parcel near Summit Avenue and Fleet Farm. This development concept, which dates back to 2006, aims to fulfill the "play" aspect of Pabst Farms' "live, work and play" development vision. The company has already sought City of Oconomowoc approvals for the project.

feed.title: Bath & Body Works wants to transform from a specialty retailer to a ‘premier global brand’
feed.summary: Bath & Body Works is undergoing a transformation from a specialty retailer to a "premier global brand" despite a decline in Q4 net sales and a forecast for continued sales decrease in 2026. The company is implementing its "Consumer First Formula" to drive sustainable growth, focusing on an Amazon launch with an updated visual identity, accelerating international store openings, and revitalizing its body care category. They also plan a significant increase in influencer marketing to enhance social media presence.

feed.title: Harley ends yearly Homecoming festival, shifts to milestone events
feed.summary: Harley-Davidson has canceled its annual Homecoming Festival in Milwaukee after three years, choosing instead to focus on milestone anniversary events. The decision follows a decline in attendance for the festival and recent leadership changes within the company. Harley-Davidson will continue to maintain a presence in Milwaukee's festival scene through a renewed partnership with Summerfest.

feed.title: Ex-Dividend Reminder: Allison Transmission Holdings, Global Industrial and Strategic Education
feed.summary: Allison Transmission Holdings (ALSN), Global Industrial Company (GIC), and Strategic Education Inc (STRA) are set to trade ex-dividend on March 9, 2026. This means investors buying shares on or after that date will not receive the upcoming dividends. The companies will pay quarterly dividends of $0.29, $0.28, and $0.60 respectively, with the stock prices expected to adjust lower by the dividend amount on the ex-dividend date.

feed.title: More Problems at Serve Robotics (SERV)
feed.summary: This article highlights further issues at Serve Robotics (SERV), an experimental company using self-driving robots for food delivery. Despite investor expectations for rapid growth, the author outlines three main problems: public opposition to sidewalk robots, poor performance and illegal operation of the robots, and restaurants dropping Serve as a partner. The piece provides examples from Chicago, Miami, and social media to illustrate these challenges, suggesting that 2026 will bring a more realistic outlook for Serve shareholders.

feed.title: UBS Raises Price Target for National Vision Holdings (EYE) to $4
feed.summary: UBS has reaffirmed its 'Buy' rating for National Vision Holdings (EYE) and increased its price target by 7.69% to $42.00 from $39.00. This adjustment reflects the analyst's confidence in the company's future performance. Several other analysts have also recently updated their ratings and price targets for National Vision Holdings, indicating varied but generally positive outlooks for the optical retailer.

feed.title: BJ’s Wholesale Club Holdings, Inc. Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Fiscal 2025 Results
feed.summary: BJ’s Wholesale Club Holdings, Inc. announced strong fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2025 results, with growth driven by membership, digital sales, and traffic. The company reported a 1.6% increase in comparable club sales and a 10.9% rise in membership fee income for the fourth quarter. It also provided fiscal 2026 guidance, projecting a 2.0% to 3.0% increase in comparable club sales (excluding gasoline) and adjusted EPS ranging from $4.40 to $4.60.

feed.title: G-III Apparel Group Announces Date for Fourth Quarter and Full Year Fiscal 2026 Results
feed.summary: G-III Apparel Group, Ltd. announced it will release its fourth quarter and full year fiscal 2026 earnings on Thursday, March 12, 2026, before market open. Management will host a conference call at 8:30 a.m. ET to discuss the results, followed by a Q&A session. The company is a global fashion leader with a portfolio of over 30 owned and licensed brands.

feed.title: The Buckle’s February Comps Grow 8 Percent
feed.summary: The Buckle, Inc. reported an 8 percent increase in comparable store sales for the four-week period ending February 28. Net sales for February rose 8.8 percent to $84.4 million compared to the previous year. The company, which operates 440 stores, will release its fourth-quarter and full-year fiscal 2025 earnings on March 13.

feed.title: How authenticity drives Angelina’s sales leadership success
feed.summary: This article highlights Angelina, a Junior Local Sales Manager at Yelp, and her journey to sales leadership success. Starting without prior sales experience, she earned multiple promotions and President's Club awards, crediting her success to empowerment, growth, and authenticity. Angelina emphasizes the importance of authenticity in leadership, creating an environment where team members feel comfortable being themselves, which builds trust and drives meaningful results.

feed.title: Bath & Body Works Says Strategy Shift Making Progress But ‘Will Take Time’
feed.summary: Bath & Body Works reported lower fourth-quarter profits and sales, and provided soft guidance for the full year. Despite the financial dip, the company's CEO, Daniel Heaf, stated that its strategy pivot to refocus on core products and drive innovation is making progress, though he acknowledges that a full transformation will take time.

feed.title: Walmart Inc Stock (WMT) Moved Down by 3.77% on Mar 5: What Investors Need To Know
feed.summary: Walmart Inc (WMT) stock declined by 3.77% on March 5, largely due to an analyst downgrade from Erste Group and persistent valuation concerns, despite the company reporting strong Q4 results that exceeded revenue and EPS expectations. Additional factors include earlier downgrades by HSBC, insider selling activities, and a broader outlook of cautious consumer spending and potential cost increases in the retail sector. The stock's technical indicators show a mixed outlook, with a neutral MACD, neutral RSI, and oversold Williams %R, while fundamental analysis highlights its industry-leading revenue and net profit but also notes analyst concerns about overvaluation and conservative guidance.

feed.title: Disguise Renews Global Rights to Minecraft Costumes
feed.summary: Disguise, Inc., the costume division of JAKKS Pacific, Inc., has renewed its global licensing agreement for Minecraft costumes and accessories. This multi-year extension follows a successful 2025 driven by "A Minecraft Movie" release, reinforcing the brand's importance in Disguise's portfolio. With a second movie slated for 2027, Disguise anticipates continued demand and opportunities for the franchise.

feed.title: April 24th Options Now Available For Tractor Supply (TSCO)
feed.summary: New options for Tractor Supply Co. (TSCO) with an April 24th expiration are now available. BNK Invest's YieldBoost formula highlights a put contract at a $46.00 strike price offering a 1.52% return if it expires worthless, and a call contract at a $53.00 strike price potentially yielding 7.77% as a covered call. These options present strategies for investors interested in purchasing TSCO shares at a discount or generating income through covered calls.

feed.title: UBS raises Ross Stores stock price target on growth outlook
feed.summary: UBS has increased its price target for Ross Stores (NASDAQ:ROST) to $208 from $199, maintaining a Neutral rating, citing the company's strong fourth-quarter 2025 report and its ability to outgrow department store peers. The firm forecasts a 6.5% five-year earnings per share compound annual growth rate, justifying a P/E ratio around 26x, though the stock currently trades at a higher P/E of 31.86, near its 52-week high. Other analysts like Bernstein SocGen Group and Wells Fargo also raised their price targets for Ross Stores following its strong Q4 fiscal 2025 results and positive outlook.

feed.title: Kroger stock pops on solid Q4 2025 earnings
feed.summary: Kroger's stock rose despite a market downturn, driven by strong Q4 2025 earnings. Excluding a one-time impairment charge, adjusted EPS grew by 9%, and both identical sales and e-commerce saw significant increases. The company also announced a new $2 billion share repurchase authorization and provided solid 2026 guidance, reinforcing confidence in its stable business model.

feed.title: April 24th Options Now Available For Lamb Weston Holdings (LW)
feed.summary: New options contracts for Lamb Weston Holdings (LW) with an April 24th expiration date have begun trading. BNK Invest's YieldBoost formula highlights a put contract at the $46.00 strike price and a call contract at the $51.00 strike price, detailing potential strategies and returns for investors interested in these options. The article provides analysis on both put and covered call strategies, including implied volatility and historical stock performance.

feed.title: Fastenal’s February: Continued Sales Acceleration Tops Expectations
feed.summary: Fastenal reported strong February sales, with total sales reaching $711 million, up 13.3% year-over-year on a daily sales basis, surpassing analyst estimates. This marks the company's best daily sales growth since October 2022 and its eighth consecutive month of double-digit growth. The acceleration occurred despite a decrease in the percentage of top national accounts showing growth, driven by strong performance in various geographies, product categories, and end markets.

feed.title: Dollar General (DG) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: Can the Stock Move Higher?
feed.summary: Dollar General (DG) is expected to beat its earnings estimates for the quarter ended January 2026, with a forecasted EPS of $1.61 and revenues of $10.78 billion. The company has a positive Earnings ESP of +5.38% and a Zacks Rank of #3, indicating a high probability of an earnings beat. Historically, Dollar General has a strong track record of surpassing EPS estimates.

feed.title: Amazon bets on health care AI with tools for patients, doctors
feed.summary: Amazon's cloud unit, Amazon Web Services (AWS), is launching new AI tools for medical practices under the name Amazon Connect Health. These tools aim to reduce administrative complexity in healthcare by generating notes from doctor-patient conversations, adding billing codes, summarizing health data, and assisting with patient identification and appointment management. This move marks Amazon's latest effort to expand its cloud-computing business into specialized workplace applications, competing with other tech giants also investing in healthcare AI.

feed.title: Food News: Chipotle Opens, Broadway Deli for Sale, Primal Closes, New Chef, New Menu, More
feed.summary: This article provides an update on various food-related news in the Saratoga area. It covers the opening of a new Chipotle location in Malta, the listing for sale of Saratoga’s Broadway Deli, the sudden closure of Primal butcher shop, updates to the menu at Front Street Deli, the appointment of a new executive chef at Panza's, and the official opening celebration of Keuka Spring Vineyards' new tasting room. Additionally, an antique sale hosted by Sundaes Best Hot Fudge Sauce owner is announced.

feed.title: Brilliant Earth Group (NASDAQ:BRLT) Announces Quarterly Earnings Results, Misses Estimates By $0.08 EPS
